  content: "The Off The Cuff Framework for video content: (1) Dumb Stuff â€” warm up, ask 'type yes if you can hear me'; (2) Introduction â€” state today's topic; (3) Offer #1 â€” 'before we get started' framework, say domain, ask viewers to type domain in comments; (4) Elaborate on the point briefly; (5) Offer #2 â€” 'before I forget' framework; (6) Insert relevant story wrapper; (7) Offer #3 â€” 'by the way' framework; (8) Main Point â€” restate your teaching; (9) Q&A â€” answer questions, make offer after each answer."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-058"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Three Offer Language Patterns: (1) 'By the way' â€” mid-content, casual redirect; (2) 'Before I forget' â€” creates urgency/casualness; (3) 'Before we get started' â€” top of video, first mention. Always say the domain name, always ask viewers to type domain in comments."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-059"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "The Vortex Engagement Flow: Cold audience sees content â†’ If engaged (watch/click/like/comment/share) â†’ Retarget with 60% content + 40% offers â†’ If they visit website â†’ Retarget multi-channel with 80% offers + 20% content â†’ If they don't click, keep showing 60/40 content/offers."
  domain: "marketing-strategy"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-060"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Competitive Research Dossier Framework: For each of top 5 competitors, document: (1) Traffic by countries (find gaps); (2) Demographics (age, gender); (3) Social traffic sources (which platforms); (4) Top content by engagement (80/20 rule); (5) Display ads running and duration; (6) Landing pages and offers; (7) Audience interests; (8) Keywords being bid on."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-061"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "IBB Cold-to-Retarget Campaign Structure: Campaign 1 (Cold) = multiple content pieces to strangers â†’ Campaign 2 (Warm) split: 40% budget showing unwatched content + 60% budget showing direct response offer ads with images. The retarget step is where conversions happen."
  domain: "paid-traffic"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-062"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Content Idea Generation System â€” 6 sources: (1) Yeah-Buts and What-Ifs from your 3 pillars; (2) Daily life/personal experience/client stories; (3) Social media comments and competitor Q&A; (4) Buzzfeed articles on your topic; (5) Reddit AMAs in your niche; (6) Google 'stories about [topic]' for wrappers."
  domain: "content-strategy"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-063"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Weekly Content Planning: Every Monday, review your scratch pad of ideas. Assign one topic per day from your 3 pillars. Each topic = one fear/objection wrapped in a fresh story. You only have to do this once or twice before it becomes habitual."
  domain: "content-strategy"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-064"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Facebook Post Structure: Subject line stating video topic â†’ Link to easy-to-remember domain â†’ Video following Off The Cuff framework â†’ In comments: post the link â†’ Ask viewers to type domain in comments for more clickable links and engagement."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "FW-FK-065"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Multi-Angle Follow-Up Sequence for selling consulting: (1) Webinar pitches application; (2) Series of 3 follow-up videos (~6 min each) re-pitching application; (3) Email with PDF link â€” subject: 'letter from the scene', body: 'not sure I have your address, click to read PDF' â€” PDF is long-form sales letter with application link."
  domain: "sales-funnels"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-066"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Simple Sales Letter Structure for strategy sessions: Headline = 'If you want [result], read this.' Body = 'Here's what I got. Here's what it'll do for you. Here's what I need you to do next.' Close = fill in application form."
  domain: "copywriting"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "FW-FK-067"
  layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS
  content: "Spy Tools Stack for Competitive Intelligence: (1) SimilarWeb â€” traffic sources, demographics, countries, social breakdown; (2) SEMrush â€” keywords, display ads, landing pages, paid competitors; (3) Social Insider â€” best posts, engagement by type, top videos; (4) Facebook Ads Library â€” current ads, duration running, geographic targeting."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

# =========================
# L5_METHODOLOGIES
# =========================

- id: "MET-FK-040"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "4-Step Competitive Takeover Methodology: Step 1 â€” List top 5 competitors and their websites. Step 2 â€” See what kind of content is working for them (use 80/20 on their videos/posts). Step 3 â€” Know what's going on behind the scenes (buy their stuff, opt into funnels, see upsells). Step 4 â€” Take all data and execute with similar-but-better content, ads, and funnels to the same audiences."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-041"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Content Video Creation Methodology (Off The Cuff): (1) Go live on Facebook even if nobody's watching â€” forces you to get good. (2) Follow the Off The Cuff grid: Dumb stuff â†’ Intro â†’ Offer 1 (before we start) â†’ Elaborate â†’ Offer 2 (before I forget) â†’ Story â†’ Offer 3 (by the way) â†’ Main point â†’ Q&A with offers. (3) Make domain easy to remember. (4) Do this every single day."
  domain: "content-creation"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "MET-FK-042"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "3-Pillar Content Strategy Methodology: (1) Draw a line from 'don't have results' to 'got results.' (2) Map the 3 big things they need to do. (3) For each big thing, list all Yeah-Buts (objections) and What-Ifs (fears). (4) Each objection/fear = one piece of content. (5) Wrap each piece in a story from 6 idea sources. (6) Publish daily, always making offer several times per video."
  domain: "content-strategy"
  source: "OT-W2"

- id: "MET-FK-043"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Competitive Research Deep-Dive Methodology: (1) Enter competitor domain in SimilarWeb â€” note countries, demographics. (2) Check social traffic breakdown (Facebook vs YouTube vs others). (3) In SEMrush, check paid keywords and display ads â€” note ads running 100+ days. (4) In Social Insider, check top 20% of video posts by engagement. (5) In Facebook Ads Library, check active ads and duration. (6) Compile all notes into Competitor Analysis Google Doc with screenshots."
  domain: "competitive-intelligence"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-044"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Geo-Fence Targeting for Brick & Mortar: (1) Get MLS listings on market 130-150 days (approaching expiration at 180 days). (2) Geo-target exact home addresses. (3) Show ads: 'Ready to finally sell your house? Call [agent].' (4) Set conversion zones at your own business location. (5) Algorithm tracks: saw ad â†’ visited your location = conversion."
  domain: "geofencing"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-045"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Search Term Retargeting for Local Business: (1) Identify high-intent search terms (e.g., 'Ford F-150'). (2) Ad network tags device IDs that searched those terms. (3) Filter by geographic radius from your location. (4) Show banner ads across all websites they visit. (5) Much cheaper than AdWords clicks but captures same intent signals."
  domain: "retargeting"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-046"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "Addressable List Retargeting Methodology: (1) Get buyer list from list broker (exact data, info USA) filtered by purchase behavior. (2) Upload addresses for geo-fencing each individual household. (3) All mobile devices at those addresses get tagged. (4) When they leave home and connect to other Wi-Fi networks, ads transfer to those devices too. (5) Track conversions when tagged device enters your physical location."
  domain: "geofencing"
  source: "OT-W1"

- id: "MET-FK-047"
  layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES
  content: "IBB for Local Business (Dentist Example): (1) Run content videos with offers: 'Call me if you want your teeth fixed.' (2) Do NOT switch to lead capture forms â€” it changes the natural sales process. (3) Track new patients/calls month-over-month, not just click attribution. (4) Most conversions come from people remembering and typing domain/calling directly, not clicking ads."

## Overview
Closers Into Leaders (CIL) is Cole Gordon's flagship comprehensive sales training program covering the complete lifecycle from individual sales mastery to building and managing high-performing sales teams. The program synthesizes Cole's experience selling $400K+/month as a solo sales rep and scaling multiple sales organizations.

## Structure
| Module | Topic | Files | Key Frameworks |
|--------|-------|-------|----------------|
| 0 | Intro/Onboarding | 7 | DPL x LTV Model, Alignment Triad, Triage Call Flow |
| 1 | Sales Fundamentals | 22 | Seven Belief Ladder, Reverse Selling System, Offer Models, Copy Platform, Inner Game |
| 2 | Client Acquisition | 52 | Pitch Codex, Outbound Messenger, Content Marketing, Pipeline Systems, Two-Step Posts |
| 3 | Closing Mastery | 37 | Belief Blueprint, Discovery Process, Pain/Doubt/Cost/Vision Questions, Transition, Hiring |
| 4 | Objections & Management | 45 | Big Three Objections, Financial Objection Process, Pacing, Big Three Management, Culture |
| 5 | Attraction Prospecting | 16 | APS System, 18 Non-CTA Categories, Other People's Traffic, CTA Offers |
| 6 | Pipeline & Follow-Up | 17 | Re-Offer Technique, Leadership Follow-Up, Two-Step Pipeline, Outbound Calls, Triage Script |
| 7 | Hiring & Team Building | 11 | New Hiring Model (Setter->Closer), Big Three, Scorecards, Onboarding/Ramp-Up |
| 8 | Management & Leadership | 9 | Rut Diagnosis, Data-Driven Decisions, Meeting Cadence, Tracking Systems |
| Calls | Coaching/Group Calls | 118 | Story Selling, Pre-Pitch, Socratic Flow, Offer Creation, Market Sophistication |

### Novos Insights Chave

| Setter-to-Closer Progression | Modelo de carreira: Setter â†’ Junior Closer â†’ Senior Closer â†’ Team Lead â†’ Sales Manager, com KPIs por estagio | Redefine team building â€” nao contrata closers prontos, desenvolve internamente |

# MOLLY PITTMAN â€” PAID TRAFFIC MASTERY 2019: DNA EXTRACTION

**Source:** 65 files across 8 modules
**Speakers:** Molly Pittman (primary), Russ Henneberry (content marketing), Mike Rhodes (Google Ads), Tom Breeze (YouTube Ads), Justin Rondo (optimization), Josh Turner (LinkedIn Ads)

---

## L1: PHILOSOPHIES (FIL-MP)

```
ID: FIL-MP-001
content: "A media buyer aggregates and purchases attention. Media buyers connect consumers with brands that may be of service or interest to their life."
domain: media-buying-identity
source_file: 2-Paid_Traffic-_The_What___Why.md

ID: FIL-MP-002
content: "None of the tactical stuff matters if you don't understand what you're actually doing, and if you don't understand that there's a human on the other side of the computer."
domain: media-buying-philosophy
source_file: 2-Paid_Traffic-_The_What___Why.md

ID: FIL-MP-003
content: "H to H, human to human. Your job is to connect to the people on the other side of that screen."
domain: media-buying-philosophy
source_file: 2-Paid_Traffic-_The_What___Why.md

ID: FIL-MP-004
content: "Media buying is a power that can be used for bad if you choose. Make sure those are messages and products and services that you feel good about inside and that you feel aligned with because it will make you a way better media buyer."
domain: media-buying-ethics
source_file: 2-Paid_Traffic-_The_What___Why.md

ID: FIL-MP-005
content: "The role of marketing is to move prospects and customers seamlessly and subtly through each stage of the value journey."
domain: marketing-philosophy
source_file: 3-The_Customer_Journey_Explained.md

ID: FIL-MP-006
content: "Think about paid traffic as a water hose â€” you can turn it on and off whenever you please. Organic traffic is like rain â€” you aren't sure when traffic will come or how consistently."
domain: paid-traffic-philosophy
source_file: 2-Paid_Traffic-_The_What___Why.md

ID: FIL-MP-007
content: "Traffic is not going to fix a bad offer. If someone doesn't want to do what you're asking them to do, your ad copy can be amazing, your creative can be awesome, but it's just not going to work."
domain: offer-first-philosophy
source_file: 1-Creating_Your_Campaign_Assets_MP4.md

ID: FIL-MP-008
content: "The number one rule of ad copy: it's not about you â€” it's about your prospects and customers."
domain: copywriting-philosophy
source_file: 4-High_Converting_Ad_Copy_MP4.md

ID: FIL-MP-009
content: "We are marketers. We are psychologists. We are researchers. We are empaths. We are humans connecting other humans. We are communicators."
domain: media-buyer-identity
source_file: Paid-Traffic-Module-1-Notes.md

ID: FIL-MP-010
content: "I care much more about the intent and interests of your market than what they look like." â€” Intent-based targeting over demographics.
domain: targeting-philosophy
source_file: 2-Customer_Avatars_MP4.md

ID: FIL-MP-011
content: "A picture says a million words. Ad creatives are SO much more important than color and design â€” they need to convey the marketing message."
domain: creative-philosophy
source_file: 5-Ad_Creatives.md

ID: FIL-MP-012
content: "Do not give up. Most traffic campaigns fail right off the bat and that's okay. You're running these campaigns to get data to then optimize to get the results you want."
domain: media-buyer-mindset
source_file: 3-Troubleshooting_Campaigns.md

ID: FIL-MP-013
content: "AdWords is the greatest direct response machine ever invented. It's a vending machine, not a slot machine â€” incredibly predictable." (Speaker: Mike Rhodes)
domain: google-ads-philosophy
source_file: 1-The_Big_Picture.md

ID: FIL-MP-014
content: "Start with the user. Understand what your customers are doing on YouTube and why they're there in the first place â€” then you can drop all the salesmanship and just start talking to them." (Speaker: Tom Breeze)
domain: youtube-ads-philosophy
source_file: 2-Your_Customers_Moment_MP4.md

ID: FIL-MP-015
content: "The main reason you have a Google AdWords account is so that you can test different things. It's a side benefit that you get all this lovely traffic." (Speaker: Mike Rhodes)
domain: testing-philosophy
source_file: 5-Writing_Great_Ads.md

ID: FIL-MP-016
content: "You don't need your page design to be beautiful. You need it to make sense and be consistent." (Speaker: Justin Rondo)
domain: landing-page-philosophy
source_file: 4-Optimizing_MP4.md

ID: FIL-MP-017
content: "Innovate, then iterate. Find the larger scale changes that make a big difference, then fine-tune." (Speaker: Justin Rondo)
domain: optimization-philosophy
source_file: 4-Optimizing_MP4.md
```

---

## L2: MENTAL MODELS (MM-MP)

```
ID: MM-MP-001
content: "Traffic Store Model â€” Think of paid traffic platforms like different stores. Google = Yellow Pages (search intent). YouTube = Television (interrupt/consume). Facebook = Modern Day Billboard (social disruption). You go to different stores depending on what you need."
domain: platform-selection
source_file: 2-The_Traffic_Store_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-002
content: "Traffic Temperature â€” Cold (never heard of you), Warm (engaged but not purchased), Hot (buyers). Overlay on Value Journey: Cold = Aware/Engage/Subscribe, Warm = Convert/Excite, Hot = Ascend/Advocate/Promote."
domain: audience-segmentation
source_file: 3-Traffic_Temperature.md

ID: MM-MP-003
content: "Customer Value Journey â€” 8-step framework: Aware â†’ Engage â†’ Subscribe â†’ Convert â†’ Excite â†’ Ascend â†’ Advocate â†’ Promote. Each step needs its own marketing campaign."
domain: customer-journey
source_file: 3-The_Customer_Journey_Explained.md

ID: MM-MP-004
content: "Before/After State â€” A great sales message simply articulates the shift or transformation from the before state to the after state. All ad copy should reflect this."
domain: copywriting-model
source_file: 4-High_Converting_Ad_Copy_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-005
content: "The 'But No One Else Would' Trick â€” For interest targeting: 'A [market enthusiast] would know [this person/brand], but no one else would.' Filters out overly broad interests and finds the avid core of your market."
domain: targeting-model
source_file: 3-Laser_Targeting.md

ID: MM-MP-006
content: "Triggering Events Model â€” For commodity products, target based on life events (job change, birth, marriage, divorce, birthday, graduation, system failure, holiday) that create windows of opportunity where prospects are far more likely to act."
domain: targeting-model
source_file: 3-Laser_Targeting.md

ID: MM-MP-007
content: "Point of No Return â€” When scaling, there's a threshold where increasing budget further will push your success metric past your benchmark. Stop scaling vertically at that point and hold the budget at its sweet spot."
domain: scaling-model
source_file: 5-Scaling.md

ID: MM-MP-008
content: "Hub and Spoke Browsing â€” Humans browse the internet by searching for relevant answers; as soon as something feels wrong ('this isn't what I was looking for'), they hit back. Maintaining ad scent prevents this bounce behavior."
domain: user-behavior
source_file: 6-Ad_Scent_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-009
content: "Relevant Score as Feedback Loop â€” Facebook's 1-10 score reflects how well your ad resonates with your audience. High share-to-like ratio (>50%) is the strongest signal. High relevant score = cheaper traffic, more reach."
domain: facebook-algorithm
source_file: 4-High_Converting_Ad_Copy_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-010
content: "Conversion Rate Dip Diagnosis â€” A dip in conversion rates comes down to only 4 things: offer issue, articulation issue, targeting issue, or page design issue." (Speaker: Justin Rondo)
domain: optimization-diagnosis
source_file: 4-Optimizing_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-011
content: "Window Shopper / In-Store Shopper / Checkout Shopper â€” YouTube audience model. Window shoppers = interested but not engaged. In-store = researching actively. Checkout = ready to buy. Each requires different video ad structure." (Speaker: Tom Breeze)
domain: youtube-audience-model
source_file: 2-Your_Customers_Moment_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-012
content: "Schema Violation â€” Do something unexpected but related to your message at the start of a video ad. Two brain effects: (1) shock/attention, (2) mind opens up for learning. Creates deep embedded memory." (Speaker: Tom Breeze)
domain: video-ad-psychology
source_file: 3-The_Perfect_Video_Ad_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-013
content: "Hero's Journey for Customer Stories â€” Position your business as Yoda/Mr. Miyagi (mentor), not Luke Skywalker (hero). The customer is the hero. Story: hero has a problem â†’ meets a mentor â†’ overcomes â†’ transformation."
domain: storytelling-model
source_file: 7-Hot_Traffic_Content_MP4.md

ID: MM-MP-014
content: "Marketing Campaign = Content + Traffic Source â€” Every campaign consists of two things: content (the vehicle) and a traffic source (the road). Simplify by answering: what's the content? Where am I sending them? What traffic source gets them there?"
domain: campaign-architecture
source_file: 3-The_Customer_Journey_Explained.md

ID: MM-MP-015
content: "Quality Score Trinity (Google) â€” Click-through rate is the strongest signal. Negative keywords improve quality score by removing junk impressions, nearly doubling CTR. Google rewards smart advertisers with cheaper clicks." (Speaker: Mike Rhodes)
domain: google-ads-optimization
source_file: 10-Getting_Advanced_MP4.md
```

---

## L3: HEURISTICS (HEUR-MP)

```
ID: HEUR-MP-001
content: "Only about 20% of campaigns I launch work right off the bat, and that is really normal."
domain: campaign-success-rate
source_file: 1-Getting_Started_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-002
content: "For cold traffic campaigns on Facebook, audience sizes should be 500K to 7 million per ad set (unless local business)."
domain: facebook-audience-sizing
source_file: 3-Laser_Targeting.md

ID: HEUR-MP-003
content: "Don't have more than 6 ads in an ad set. Facebook just can't optimize for more than that."
domain: facebook-campaign-structure
source_file: 3-Understanding_Campaign_Structure_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-004
content: "Conversion objective needs more than 50 conversions per week for Facebook to optimize properly. Audience sizes should be more than 5,000 people."
domain: facebook-optimization-thresholds
source_file: 3-Understanding_Campaign_Structure_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-005
content: "When scaling vertically, do not increase your budget more than 50% every 7 days."
domain: scaling-budget
source_file: 5-Scaling.md

ID: HEUR-MP-006
content: "Don't have an ad set with less than $20/day budget. Recommended starting budget: $100-$200/day on a campaign to get good data quickly."
domain: facebook-budget
source_file: 4-Placements__Bidding____Budget_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-007
content: "A relevant score of 4 and above is good. Below 4, question if your targeting is off or your copy isn't resonating."
domain: facebook-relevant-score
source_file: 2-Analyzing_Data_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-008
content: "Don't let frequency exceed 10. Effective frequency is around 7."
domain: facebook-ad-frequency
source_file: 2-Analyzing_Data_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-009
content: "A unique click-through rate over 1% is good/thumbs up."
domain: facebook-ctr-benchmark
source_file: 2-Analyzing_Data_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-010
content: "Homepage conversion rate: 2-3%. Squeeze page with good offer: 40-80% conversion rate. The difference is eliminating leaks (no other directions to go)."
domain: landing-page-conversion
source_file: 6-Warm_Traffic_Content_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-011
content: "If sharing percentage is over 50% compared to likes (100 likes, 50+ shares), Facebook considers your ad exceptionally good."
domain: facebook-social-proof
source_file: 4-High_Converting_Ad_Copy_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-012
content: "Facebook tracking data is usually within 10-20% of accuracy. Always cross-reference with Google Analytics."
domain: tracking-accuracy
source_file: 6-Tracking___Campaign_Launch.md

ID: HEUR-MP-013
content: "Keep ad image text below 20% of the total image area. Facebook may restrict reach otherwise."
domain: facebook-creative-rules
source_file: 2-Facebook_Compliance_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-014
content: "The average person watches over 40 minutes of content per YouTube session â€” longer than the average TV show." (Speaker: Tom Breeze)
domain: youtube-consumption
source_file: 2-The_Traffic_Store_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-015
content: "Facebook has 50,000+ data points on each user. Over 2 billion active monthly users."
domain: facebook-data-scale
source_file: 2-The_Traffic_Store_MP4.md

ID: HEUR-MP-016
content: "About 50% of interest ideas from research will match up with actual targetable Facebook interests."
domain: targeting-research

Level 3: No page conversions â†’ check ad scent (design/offer/copy congruency), optimize landing page per Justin Rondo process. ## L1: PHILOSOPHIES (FIL-MP-600 to FIL-MP-649)

```
ID: FIL-MP-600
content: "Our goal is to really connect consumers with brands that might be of service or interest to their life. Our goal is not to make as much money as we possibly and humanly can in the next 30 days."
domain: media-buying-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-601
content: "If you really do come from this or come at this from a place of true service and wanting to connect people with things that are going to help them, all of this will come easier to you and you will in turn make that money that you're looking to make, but it will be much more sustainable and much less stressful."
domain: media-buying-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-602
content: "A media buyer simply aggregates and purchases attention. We are aggregating attention, which is the same as every other discipline in marketing, but we are willing to purchase it. And we have the ability because we're purchasing it, we can get very targeted and very specific."
domain: media-buying-definition
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-603
content: "Every human being, every consumer that's going to see the ads that you run â€” that ad has the opportunity to change their day in some way. It could affect their mood. It could make them feel happy, or it could make them feel broken."
domain: ethical-advertising
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-604
content: "Media buying is similar to golf â€” it's so mental. Usually where I see a media buyer start to fail or fail right out of the gate, it's because of: the fear of failure, money issues/scarcity mindset, or a need for validation through the success of campaigns."
domain: media-buyer-mindset
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-605
content: "The people that I see struggle are the ones that have lost sight of everything on this list and they're so focused on the tactics that they forgot why they're doing this in the first place."
domain: media-buyer-mindset
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-606
content: "I've met lots of great button clickers who are way better tactically in these platforms than I am, but I'm able to get better results. And the reason for that is their mindset."
domain: media-buyer-differentiation
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-607
content: "Your ability to be a good marketer has as much to do with your ability to put yourself in someone else's shoes as it does any of the other stuff I'm going to teach you."
domain: empathy-in-marketing
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-608
content: "We are in the beginning of this digital age. Human behavior is on our side. Every day that goes by, people are buying more stuff on the Internet. In 50 years we'll look back and think, oh my goodness, 2020 was just the beginning of this."
domain: digital-advertising-opportunity
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-609
content: "This is about direct response advertising. I'm all about building a brand but I am just not one to do advertising for the sake of advertising. I want to make sure we're able to measure very clear results from what we're doing."
domain: direct-response-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_2_Part_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-610
content: "True scale in a business comes from introducing more acquisition funnels. Not from hacking ads manager."
domain: scaling-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_2_Part_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-611
content: "A lot of us looking for scale from paid traffic, we're looking for scale inside of ads manager. While that might be the case, there's so much more scale available by introducing new front-end offers to your market."
domain: scaling-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_2_Part_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-612
content: "Facebook first and foremost really needs people and consumers to enjoy spending time on their platform. So action rate is all about social proof and the interaction on your ads."
domain: platform-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-613
content: "Campaign structure used to be very complicated because the platform was not that smart. Now, over the last 10 years, if you're over-testing or your campaign structure is too complicated, it can really negatively affect your overall results."
domain: campaign-simplification
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-614
content: "Facebook is more like a billboard. People aren't searching Facebook with intent. We are showing up as advertisers in people's news feeds. Because of that, we need to really relate to their lives."
domain: platform-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_4.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-615
content: "I never know what this person [avatar] is actually going to look like. Remove demographics from your brain and start thinking deeper. Facebook has so much interest-based data."
domain: avatar-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_4.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-616
content: "This is my top secret sauce as a media buyer â€” my ability to just put myself in my avatar's shoes and spend time really finding out where they hang out. Usually that has nothing to do with gender or age."
domain: avatar-research
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_4.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-617
content: "Optimization is about discovering what's wrong with the campaign, pinpointing what we think is wrong, coming up with a hypothesis and a plan and really a way to fix what we think failed. This is not black and white."
domain: optimization-philosophy
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_7.md (Session 8 folder)
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FIL-MP-618
content: "Some of us are going to have to be willing to lose a little bit of money up front, depending on your business type, to truly get paid acquisition to work at scale."
domain: customer-acquisition-economics

speaker: Molly Pittman

## L2: MENTAL MODELS (MM-MP-600 to MM-MP-654)

```
ID: MM-MP-600
content: "Traffic System Model â€” A system of campaigns running across multiple traffic platforms that are built to automate a customer journey and scale a business. Like a physical store, there are multiple entry points to purchase."
domain: traffic-systems
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-601
content: "Alan's Boots Model â€” 6 entry points for a brick-and-mortar: (1) walk by and browse but don't buy, (2) walk by and purchase, (3) search online and find you, (4) remember a previous visit and return, (5) repeat customer for new product, (6) referral from existing customer."
domain: customer-journey-mapping
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-602
content: "Facebook Total Value Score â€” Your rank in the auction is determined by three components: (1) Estimated Action Rate (social proof/engagement), (2) User Value (post-click experience), (3) Bidding (what you told Facebook you're willing to pay)."
domain: facebook-algorithm
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-603
content: "The Auction Mental Model â€” Think of a numbered list ranking advertisers. Those ranking highest get cheapest traffic, most reach, and best results. Your score changes constantly based on data flowing back to Facebook."
domain: facebook-algorithm
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-604
content: "Splintering Model â€” The idea that each offer step is a very small piece of the next step you're asking them to take. Lead magnet â†’ tripwire â†’ core offer, where each builds logically on the previous."
domain: funnel-design
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_2_Part_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-605
content: "Carpenter Model for Campaign Structure â€” Like a carpenter who gets different tools for different projects. Every campaign has different objectives, budgets, and structures based on its purpose in the traffic system."
domain: campaign-structure
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-606
content: "Three Campaign Types Model â€” Every Facebook campaign you'll ever build is one of three types: (1) Test Campaign (proof of concept), (2) Scale Campaign (high volume results), (3) Retargeting Campaign (warm audiences)."
domain: campaign-structure
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-607
content: "Market-Centric vs Product-Centric Business Model â€” Market-centric businesses serve one general market (1-2 avatars). Product-centric businesses have products that appeal to multiple different markets (4+ avatars). This determines your entire targeting strategy."

source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_4.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-608
content: "Copy and Creative Funnel Depth Model â€” As you go deeper in the funnel (cold â†’ warm â†’ hot), copy shifts from education/content â†’ benefits/value â†’ objection handling/scarcity. Creative shifts from broad lifestyle â†’ product-focused â†’ urgency."

source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MM-MP-609

ID: MM-MP-610

ID: MM-MP-611
content: "Facebook's Trust Problem Model â€” Only 3% of consumers trust Facebook, but it's the #1 platform advertisers spend on. Facebook compensates by aggressively policing advertiser behavior, ad policy, and user experience."

source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md

ID: MM-MP-613
content: "Seven Acquisition Funnel Types: (1) Traditional 3-Step (lead magnet â†’ tripwire â†’ core offer), (2) Webinar, (3) Free Video Series, (4) Lead Magnet â†’ Core Offer, (5) Lead Magnet â†’ Discovery Call, (6) Free Service â†’ Upsell, (7) Free Item â†’ Upsell"

ID: MM-MP-614

```
ID: FW-MP-600
content: "Traffic System Planning Framework â€” A bird's-eye-view planning sheet with columns: Existing/New, Offer Description, Success Metric, Benchmark Cost, Actual Cost, Volume of Results (Goal vs Actual). Each row is a campaign step in the customer journey."

ID: FW-MP-603

speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: FW-MP-605
content: "Facebook Total Value Optimization Framework â€” To rank highest in auction, optimize all three components: (1) Action Rate: maximize social proof and engagement, (2) User Value: optimize post-click experience (landing page, site speed, conversion rate), (3) Bidding: choose correct objective and bid strategy."

content: "BOOM Interest Targeting Framework â€” Use Facebook Audience Insights + Google to find interests. ID: FW-MP-608
content: "Ad Copy Framework â€” Hook (first 1-2 sentences that stop the scroll) â†’ Story/Education (value that builds trust) â†’ Offer (what you want them to do) â†’ CTA (clear directive). Hook types: Question, Bold Statement, Statistic, Testimonial, Before/After."

content: "Ad Creative Testing Framework â€” Test these variables in order: (1) Format (video vs image vs carousel), (2) Hook/thumbnail, (3) Length (short vs long), (4) Style (UGC vs polished vs screenshot). Never test more than one variable at a time."

(4) Upload ad variations. (5) Set budget conservatively to start. (6) Double-check UTM parameters. (7) Launch and don't touch for 24-48 hours."

ID: FW-MP-611
content: "Troubleshooting Diagnostic Framework â€” Step 1: Check if getting reach (if no: budget, audience size, policy issue). Step 2: Check CTR (if low: creative/copy problem). Layer 3 (14-day): Cart abandoners â†’ show scarcity/discount. Layer 4 (28-day+): Email list/past buyers â†’ show new offers/cross-sells."

ID: FW-MP-613
content: "CBO Scaling Framework (Nehal Kazim) â€” Step 1: Start with 3-5 proven ad sets in CBO. Step 2: Use minimum/maximum spend limits per ad set (20% floor, no ceiling). ## L5: METHODOLOGIES (MET-MP-600 to MET-MP-639)

```
ID: MET-MP-600
content: "Traffic System Building Methodology â€” 8-week process: Week 1: Mindset + Traffic System Planning. Week 2: Choose Acquisition Funnels. Week 3: Structure Facebook Campaigns. Week 4: Avatar Research + Targeting. Week 5: Write Ad Copy. Week 6: Create Ad Creatives. Week 7: Launch Campaigns. Week 8: Optimize + Troubleshoot. Then retargeting, scaling, multi-platform expansion."
domain: traffic-system-building
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_1.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-601
content: "Test Campaign Setup Methodology â€” Step 1: Define one success metric (CPL, CPA, ROAS). Step 2: Create 5-10 ad sets with different targeting (interests, lookalikes). Step 3: Set equal budget per ad set ($10-50/day each). Step 4: Create 5-6 identical ads per ad set (3 copy variations x 2 creative variations). Step 5: Launch and wait 3-5 days minimum before making changes. Step 6: Evaluate which ad sets and ads performed best. Step 7: Kill losers, keep winners for scale campaign."
domain: testing-methodology
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-602
content: "Scale Campaign Setup Methodology â€” Step 1: Take winning 2-3 ads from test (use post IDs for social proof). Step 2: Create CBO campaign with 3-5 ad sets. Step 3: Combine similar performing interests into larger audiences (5M+). Step 4: Add 1-2 fresh ads alongside proven ones. Step 5: Set CBO budget at 2-3x what total was working in test. Step 6: Let run 3-5 days, then increase budget 20-50% every 2-3 days."
domain: scaling-methodology
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_3.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-603
content: "Avatar Creation Methodology â€” Step 1: Name the avatar (give them a persona name). Step 2: Define psychographics (what keeps them up at night, what they desire, daily frustrations). Step 3: Map their information diet (podcasts, blogs, books, YouTube channels). Step 4: Identify brands/tools they use. Step 5: List authority figures they follow. Step 6: Determine communities they belong to. Step 7: Use all of this for targeting research."
domain: avatar-creation
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_4.md
speaker: Molly Pittman/Russ Henneberry

ID: MET-MP-604
content: "Interest Targeting Research Methodology â€” Step 1: Open Facebook Audience Insights. Step 2: Enter seed interest from avatar research. Step 3: Note page likes, demographics, other interests that appear. Step 4: Google '[avatar interest] + magazine/blog/podcast/tool'. Step 5: Check if each result exists as a Facebook interest (search in ad set targeting). Step 6: Group interests into themed ad sets (3-5 interests per ad set in test, 5-10 in scale). Step 7: Build lookalike audiences from top converters."
domain: targeting-research
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_4.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-605
content: "Ad Copy Writing Methodology â€” Step 1: Define the hook (question, statement, stat, testimonial). Step 2: Write 3 variations of the hook. Step 3: For each hook, write the body (education/story that connects hook to offer). Step 4: Insert the offer (what they get, what it costs, why now). Step 5: End with clear CTA (Learn More, Shop Now, Sign Up). Step 6: Write short version (1-2 sentences + CTA) and long version (full story + CTA) of each."
domain: ad-copywriting
source_file: transcription_mp4_TMTP_Session_5__Writing_Compelling_Ad_Copy.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-606
content: "Creative Testing Methodology â€” Step 1: Start with 3 formats (1 video, 1 image, 1 carousel). Step 2: For video, test 2 hooks (first 3 seconds different). Step 3: For images, test lifestyle vs product vs UGC. Step 4: Put all in test campaign with proven copy. Step 5: After 3-5 days, identify winning format. Step 6: Create 3-5 variations of winning format with different angles."
domain: creative-testing
source_file: transcription_mp4_TMTP_Mentorship_Fall_2020_Session_6_High_Converting_Ad_Creatives.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-607
content: "Campaign Optimization Decision Tree â€” IF no reach: check audience size, budget, policy violations â†’ duplicate and restart. IF reach but no clicks: creative/hook problem â†’ test new creatives. IF clicks but no conversions: landing page problem â†’ optimize landing page first. IF conversions but CPA too high: targeting problem â†’ refine audiences. IF CPA good but low volume: scale problem â†’ increase budget or add new ad sets."
domain: optimization
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_7.md (Session 8)
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-608
content: "Retargeting Campaign Setup Methodology â€” Step 1: Define retargeting tiers (site visitors 3-day, 7-day, 14-day, 28-day; cart abandoners; email list). Step 2: Create different ad messaging per tier (more educational at top, more urgency at bottom). Step 3: Use reach objective for small audiences (<50K), conversion objective for larger audiences. Step 4: Budget 20-30% of total ad spend on retargeting. Step 5: Exclude purchasers from all non-post-purchase campaigns."
domain: retargeting
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_9_-_Retargetting_.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-609
content: "Horizontal Scaling Implementation â€” Step 1: List all audiences that worked in test (interests + lookalikes). Step 2: Research adjacent interests for each winner. Step 3: Create new ad sets targeting adjacent interests. Step 4: Simultaneously test new creative angles on winning audiences. Step 5: Every 1-2 weeks, add 1-2 new ad sets with fresh targeting. Step 6: Expand to new platforms (Google remarketing first, then YouTube, then Google Search)."
domain: scaling
source_file: transcription_mp4_4_23_2020_-_TMTP_Session_11.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-610
content: "Vertical Scaling Implementation (CBO) â€” Step 1: Confirm campaign has been profitable for 5+ days. Step 2: Increase budget 20-50% (not more). Step 3: Wait 2-3 days for re-optimization. Step 4: If still profitable, increase again. Step 5: If CPA spikes, reduce budget 10-20% and wait. Step 6: Continue until diminishing returns, then shift to horizontal scaling."
domain: scaling
source_file: transcription_mp4_CBO_Scaling_with_Nehal_Kazim.md
speaker: Nehal Kazim/Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-611
content: "Google Ads Setup Methodology (Mike Rhodes) â€” Step 1: Start with branded search campaigns (cheapest, highest intent). Step 2: Add competitor keyword campaigns. Step 3: Add Smart Shopping campaigns for e-commerce. Step 4: Add non-branded search for high-intent keywords. Step 5: Layer in Display for remarketing. Step 6: Add YouTube for top of funnel."
domain: google-ads
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_14.md
speaker: Mike Rhodes

ID: MET-MP-612
content: "YouTube Ad Creation Methodology (Tom Breeze) â€” Step 1: Script first 5 seconds as pattern interrupt (question, shocking stat, demonstrate result). Step 2: Identify with viewer ('If you're a [avatar] who struggles with [problem]...'). Step 3: Teach/demonstrate for 30-60 seconds. Step 4: Present offer with clear CTA. Step 5: Create 3 versions with different hooks. Step 6: Test as TrueView in-stream targeting custom intent audiences."
domain: youtube-ads
source_file: transcription_mp4_Train_My_Traffic_Person_Session_13.md
speaker: Tom Breeze

ID: MET-MP-613
content: "Account Recovery Methodology â€” Step 1: Screenshot everything (ads, copy, creatives, account structure). Step 2: Click Request Appeal in red banner. Step 3: Write friendly, empathetic message assuming mistake. Step 4: Check facebook.com/support daily. Step 5: If no response in 5-6 days, submit again. Step 6: Simultaneously set up backup ad account with shared pixel. Step 7: Try facebook.com/business/help chat. Step 8: If all fails, go to business.facebook.com/account-quality for diagnostic info."
domain: account-management
source_file: transcription_mp4_Facebook_Ad_Policy.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-614
content: "Media Buyer Client Acquisition Methodology â€” Step 1: Build 2-3 case studies (even from your own campaigns). Step 2: Create a case study lead magnet. Step 3: Run ads to generate leads. Step 4: Offer free audit/discovery call. Step 5: Present findings + proposal. Step 6: Start with 3-month contracts minimum. Step 7: Upsell to other services as you prove results."
domain: media-buyer-business
source_file: transcription_mp4_TMTP_Session_15.md
speaker: Molly Pittman

ID: MET-MP-615
content: "E-commerce Email Marketing Methodology (Ezra) â€” Step 1: Set up 5 automations (welcome, cart abandon, post-purchase, win-back, browse abandon). Step 2: Send 2-3 campaigns per week minimum. Step 3: Content mix: 60% value/content, 30% promotion, 10% engagement. Step 4: Segment by purchase behavior (buyers, non-buyers, VIPs). Step 5: Test subject lines aggressively. Step 6: Goal: email generates 30-40% of total revenue."
domain: email-marketing
source_file: transcription_mp4_Bonus__Ezra_Firestone_on_Email_Marketing.md

## QUEM SOU EU

I'm Molly Pittman â€” I started as an intern at DigitalMarketer in 2012, answering customer support tickets and literally learning marketing from scratch. Within 4 years I became VP of Marketing, running all paid traffic for a company doing $30M+/year. I didn't have a marketing degree. I didn't have connections. I just had an obsession with understanding HOW paid traffic actually works â€” not the hacks, not the tricks, but the SYSTEMS underneath.

I left DigitalMarketer to build Train My Traffic Person because I saw the same problem everywhere: businesses spending thousands on ads with zero system. They'd boost a post, pray, and wonder why nothing worked. I wanted to fix that.

I've spent over $100 million on paid advertising across Facebook, Google, YouTube, and LinkedIn. I've trained thousands of media buyers. I teach from a place of service â€” H to H, human to human. Traffic is not about manipulating people. It's about connecting the right message with the right person at the right time.

My core truth: **Traffic is a water hose. You point it at whatever you want to grow. But if the garden is dead, no amount of water will fix it.**

^[PTM/Introduction, PTS/Module-1, TMTP/Module-1]

But if you're going straight for the discount with the first message you communicate, you will attract the bottom feeders." | domain: pricing-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-1_The_Content_Planning_Matrix.md
- FIL-DM-106: "The most important thing is to acquire the customer first. CPA comes back in, acquire them as cost-effective as you can, get the AOV as high as you can, and then work on lifetime value." | domain: ecommerce-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_2_1-1.md
- FIL-DM-107: "You can beat your competition by either outspending them because your CPA is so good, or you can outlive them because your LTV is so good." | domain: competitive-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_2_1-1.md
- FIL-DM-108: "Remove the emotion and guesswork. Use data. Obviously have a think about the impact on brand as well. But Facebook is all data driven, rely on the data." | domain: data-driven-marketing | source: transcription_mp4_04-Creative_Fishing_Strategy_MP4.md
- FIL-DM-109: "Nudge is moving someone from one point to another... influencing the choice that someone has, but in a way that does not force them to do so." | domain: persuasion-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_01-3N_Ads_Intro_MP4.md
- FIL-DM-110: "When people buy functionally, they think clearly â€” do I need this? Yes or no. But when you work on the emotional, that's how you actually bring people into your funnel." | domain: buyer-psychology | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-1_The_Content_Planning_Matrix.md
- FIL-DM-111: "If they cry, they'll buy. If there's an emotional angle, people are more likely to take an interest." | domain: emotional-selling | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-1_The_Content_Planning_Matrix.md
- FIL-DM-112: "Absolutely avoid overconfidence â€” it is one of the most dangerous forms of revenue loss." â€” on automation rules | domain: automation-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_Automation_Accelerator.md
- FIL-DM-113: "Cycle your visuals more than your copy. If your creative is not getting people to stop scrolling, then the copy is redundant." | domain: creative-hierarchy | source: transcription_mp4_02-N1_-_Stop_The_Scroll_MP4.md
- FIL-DM-114: "There should be no reason at all that you are never testing anything on Facebook. Your testing should literally be seven days of the week." | domain: always-be-testing | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_Graduation_Framework_MP4.md

- FIL-DM-116: "Marketing principles don't change. The human mind hasn't changed fundamentally in the digital age. People who repackage it now for the digital age are just taking what was done before and updating it." | domain: marketing-fundamentals | source: transcription_mp4_04-N3_-_Convert_MP4.md
- FIL-DM-117: "I focus on quality of clicks, not on volume. That for me is a way of maximizing the data and the performance." | domain: traffic-quality | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-1_The_Content_Planning_Matrix.md

## L2 MENTAL-MODELS

- MM-DM-101: "Oceans, Boats, and Hooks" â€” audiences are oceans, ad sets are boats (budget = boat size), ads are hooks. Multiple boats in same ocean = H scaling. Bigger boat = V scaling. | domain: scaling-mental-model | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_Scaling_MP4.md
- MM-DM-102: "AIDA Model for Ad Copy" â€” Attention (big bold statement), Interest (hook/intrigue), Desire (new state/outcome), Action (tell them what to do). Applied to every ad format. | domain: copywriting-model | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-2_-_About_the_5W_avatar.md
- MM-DM-103: "Awareness x Intent Matrix" â€” 3x3 grid mapping customer awareness (low/mid/high) against purchase intent (low/mid/high). Each cell dictates campaign type, creative angle, and audience targeting. | domain: audience-planning | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-1_The_Content_Planning_Matrix.md
- MM-DM-104: "ROAS Tree" â€” ROAS = CPA x AOV. CPA = CPC x CR%. AOV = ASP x QTY. Only two metrics influence CPA: cost per click and conversion rate. | domain: ecommerce-metrics | source: transcription_mp4_2_1-1.md
- MM-DM-105: "Maslow's Hierarchy applied to ads" â€” Products at bottom of pyramid (food, sleep) need less explanation; products at top (self-esteem, achievement) require longer funnels and more emotional copy. | domain: buyer-psychology | source: transcription_mp4_03-N2_-_Engage.md
- MM-DM-106: "3R Branding Framework" â€” Relationship (matchmaking audience + ad), Rapport (building connection through funnel), Relevance (right ad at right time). | domain: branding-framework | source: transcription_pdf_1_3.md
- MM-DM-107: "Staircase Scaling Method" â€” increment 20% every other day until confident, then up to 20% twice per day. 10% decrease as punishment signal. Compound 20% daily = 300% in 5 days. | domain: scaling-model | source: transcription_mp4_2-The_V_Scaling_Strategy_MP4.md
- MM-DM-108: "Hook, Line, and Sinker" â€” if you grab attention (hook), peak interest (line), then create desire (sinker), you've absolutely captured them. | domain: engagement-model | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-2_-_About_the_5W_avatar.md
- MM-DM-109: "Trial Close concept (from Russell Brunson)" â€” get the prospect to say 'yes' mentally to as many non-controversial statements as possible before the action. | domain: persuasion-technique | source: transcription_mp4_1_3-3_5W_Avatar_Build___Ad_Copy_Mastery.md
- MM-DM-110: "Inverted Pyramid for Copy" â€” fewest words at top (headline), slightly more in sub-headline, expanding into body. Mirrors newspaper structure. Inspired by John Caples and David Ogilvy. | domain: copywriting-structure | source: transcription_mp4_03-N2_-_Engage.md
- MM-DM-111: "Appeal to emotional state, speak to functional state" â€” hook deep emotional response first, then deliver functional details. Most people go straight functional and wonder why ads fail. | domain: creative-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_03-N2_-_Engage.md

## DEPESH MANDALIA -- DNA COGNITIVE SCHEMA EXTRACTION

### SOURCE: 7-Figure Facebook Ads Playbook (5 modules, 13 files read)

---

## 1. THE 3N ADS FORMULA (Framework)

**Depesh's terminology:** "3N Ads Formula" = "3 Nudge Ads Formula"

**Core concept:** Winning the newsfeed through visual persuasion and behavioral nudging, not through technical optimization. Based on behavioral economics concept of "nudge" -- influencing choice without force. Depesh's definition: "Influence via non-coercive behavior. That's a flash way of saying get your way without force."

### N1 -- STOP THE SCROLL (First Nudge)

**Key principle:** "If you cannot get them to stop the scroll, then the first part of your advertising journey has been a failure. You can forget everything else -- even winning the auction, having great copy, great call to action, landing page, even a great product."

**Hierarchy of importance:** Visual > Copy. "Cycle your visuals more than your copy. If your creative is not getting people to stop in their scroll in their newsfeed, then the copy is redundant."

**Techniques (Depesh's exact terms):**
- **"Pop the ad"** -- Add contrasting/unnatural colors to make ads stand out. Not all technicolor, but subtle color pops
- **Product color contrast** -- Use product colors (e.g. yellow bananas) to create natural visual contrast
- **Grayscale technique** -- Works for lifestyle/people shots, but use sparingly ("works when you use it sparingly and you use it for the right shot")
- **Cropping and spacing** -- Use empty space strategically; bad cropping creates "dissidence" (friction)
- **Natural vs staged** -- Avoid overly staged/sowsy shots; use relaxed, relatable people
- **Eye contact** -- "She's looking at you -- it just makes you draw your attention onto her eyes" (psychological pull)
- **Thumbnail supremacy** -- "The thumbnail is absolutely more important than the video itself" for video ads

**Analogy:** London Underground escalator -- ads lining walls, what makes you stop and look?

### N2 -- ENGAGE (Second Nudge)

**Core principle:** "Take the customer from their goal to yours, one nudge at a time."

**Key heuristic:** "You've got literally a few seconds to get them to take note of your ad and then a few seconds if that to get them to engage."

**TV Ad Adaptation Rule:** Depesh ran a TV ad on Facebook -- "We took the end frames, the end slates of the video and brought them to the front." Deliver the payload FIRST on social (opposite of TV where audience is captive).

**Copy Engagement Approaches (test all):**
1. **Spark curiosity** -- Identify and solve a problem ("If your dog is as cheeky as Charlie...")
2. **Be controversial** -- "Don't sit on the fence of anything. Either be for or against, be supportive or destructive" ("It might be your fault")
3. **Relate and befriend** -- Softer approach ("We know how struggling with X can be, we can help")
4. **Customer state matching** -- Copy reflects funnel position (cold/warm/hot/existing)
5. **Urgency** -- "Limited to the first 100 customers" -- use sparingly, in the right moment, caps only for promotions

**Awareness Matrix (4 quadrants):**
| Customer State | Approach |
|---|---|
| Aware of product, aware of solution | Price/promotion (hardest to differentiate) |
| Aware of product, NOT aware of your solution | Convince why YOUR solution is best |
| NOT aware of product, aware they need solution | Product demos, expertise |
| NOT aware of problem, NOT aware of product | Must convince problem exists first (Henry Ford analogy) |

**Engagement Triggers to identify in your customer:**
- Goals (what they're trying to get)
- Struggles (what blocks them)
- False beliefs (mindset gaps to bridge)
- Opportunity cost of inaction
- Impulsiveness (works for low-ticket: "$20-$30 products don't need much thinking")

**Maslow's Hierarchy applied to ads:** Lower pyramid (physical needs) = less copy needed, product self-explanatory. Higher pyramid (self-esteem, achievement) = longer copy, longer funnels, more storytelling, email capture journeys.

**Appeal vs Connect principle:** "Appeal to their emotional state, but then speak to their functional state." Emotional = deeper brain response, more likely to hook. Functional = the CTA/mechanism. "A lot of people go straight for the functional -- they miss the emotional out and then wonder why their ad doesn't work."

**Copy Structure (Inverted Pyramid from Ogilvy/Caples):**
1. Headline: fewest words possible (hook)
2. Sub-headline: a few more nuggets/nudges
3. First expansion: chunky information
4. Main content: full journey

**Long copy stance:** "Don't be afraid of long copy... But the most important thing is the first few lines. If you don't get those first few lines right, no one's going to click or read more."

**Tool recommendation:** Creative Hub for mock-ups and inspiration

### N3 -- CONVERT (Third Nudge)

**Key principle:** "Tell people what you want them to do" -- explicit CTAs like "Pick yours now" with emoji + link

**Social proof heuristic:** 7,500+ likes creates curiosity ("over 7,000 people liked this -- maybe there's something in it for me"). BUT -- do NOT build social proof from cheap CPM countries: "It starts to dirty your page." Run page post engagement campaigns instead.

**Clarity rule:** "Be clear on what's coming next" -- tell them what happens after the click ("I'll email you my 49 tips")

**Urgency mechanism:** Combine clear CTA with bonus for acting today ("Plus act today and receive a bonus growth swipe file")

**CTA hierarchy:** Softer CTAs (learn more, watch more) for cold/top-of-funnel. Harder CTAs (book now, sign up) for warm/retargeting.

**Customer barrier pre-emption (from Ogilvy):** "Write down all the customer barriers. What are they likely to say no to?" Then pre-emptively address them in copy. "Attack is the best form of defense."

---

## 2. STAR TESTING METHOD (Methodology)

**Acronym:** S.T.A.R. = Setup, Test, Analyze, Repeat

**Steps:**
1. **SETUP** -- Prioritize test ideas, build the test
   - Maintain a backlog of test ideas (Trello/Kanban recommended)
   - Weekly or fortnightly: prioritize top 3-4 tests from backlog
   - Move to "Working in Progress" when building
2. **TEST** -- Run the test live
   - Move card to "Live" column
   - Track which tests are running at any given time
   - "On Hold" column for paused tests (site down, FB issues)
3. **ANALYZE** -- Not just "did it work" but "WHY did it work or not work"
   - Put data, reports, attachments in test card
   - Understand causal factors, not just outcomes
4. **REPEAT** -- Take learnings into next test cycle
   - Move to "Winner" or "Loser" column
   - Date tests and make them searchable
   - Feed learnings back into next Setup phase

**Key mindset:** "It's something to get your mindset around more than the actual process or the tool." The framework matters more than Trello.

**Logging discipline:** "When Q4 comes around again, you can go back into your test log and actually see what works."

---

## 3. SCALING STRATEGIES (4 Methodologies)

### 3A. V SCALING STRATEGY (Vertical Scaling)

**Definition:** Incrementally increase budget on a single winning ad set.

**Core heuristic:** 20% max per increment, maximum twice per day.

**Depesh's "Staircase Scaling Method":**
- Increment 20% every other day until confident
- When confident: increase 20% twice per day (morning based on prior day; afternoon if metrics are good)
- **Time cutoff:** Never increase budget after 2-3pm ("the later you increase your budgets the more chance Facebook will waste your spend")
- **Punishment signal:** 10% decrease when performance drops. "A 10% decrease tells the ad set that things are not going well -- it's almost like a punishment signal for Facebook and it ends up in most cases improving performance."

**Compound effect insight:** "20% a day doesn't sound a lot but compound increase is 300% [in 5 days]. If I said I'm going to increase budgets by 300% in five days you'd say that's crazy -- but actually I've done it here profitably."

**When to use:** New product, new audience, new creative -- when you want controlled scaling with confidence.

**Creative refresh trigger:** Frequency increases during V scaling = time for new creatives. "They may just need different messages."

**Key metrics to watch:** CPA, ROAS, CPC, CTR, delivery insights (audience overlap)

**Real example:** Client goal $25 CPA, 10K/day within 30 days. Started at hundreds/day, used V scaling to reach $4,500+/day while maintaining CPA targets. When CPA drifted, reduced spend, CPA improved, then scaled back up.

**Post-purchase upsell insight:** "Think about another sale as soon as they make that initial purchase" -- real CPA is much lower when factoring one-time offers post-purchase.

### 3B. H SCALING STRATEGY (Horizontal Scaling)

**Definition:** Duplicate winning ad set sideways with slight variations.

**Core mechanism:** Copy winning ad set, distribute budget across duplicates, change ONE variable per duplicate.

**Variables to test per duplicate:**
- Manual bid levels (1x, 2x, 3x target CPA)
- Placements (Instagram vs Facebook separate)
- Devices (mobile vs desktop)
- Landing pages
- Creatives

**Overlap heuristic:** "I don't want my ad sets overlapping more than 30%." Check via delivery insights.

**"Oceans, boats, and hooks" analogy:** Ocean = audience. Boats = ad sets. Hooks = ads. Duplicating = putting more boats in the same ocean.

**Cut losses early rule:** "If I know by 1pm the CPA should be X based on the winning ad set, and my duplicates are nowhere near that, I'm going to pause them."

**Insurance policy principle:** "Your original ad set becomes your insurance policy." Never touch the original budget when H scaling.

**Manual bid testing approach within H scaling:**
- Test 30, 50, 75, 90 (for a $30 CPA target)
- Start each at $100/day
- Scale the winning bid level aggressively
- Real example: $90 max bid (3x target) performed best, scaled to $1,200/day

**Key rule:** "Change one variable at a time and measure it."

### 3C. VHV SCALING STRATEGY (Vertical-Horizontal-Vertical)

**Depesh's preferred method:** "This is my preferred method of scaling any kind of account."

**Sequence:**
1. **V phase:** Start with vertical scaling to prove audience scalability. 20% increments, 5-7 days, prove consistency
2. **H phase:** Duplicate winning ad set sideways. One exact copy (no changes). Additional copies with ONE change each:
   - Manual bid at 1x, 2x, 3x target CPA
   - Placement splits (Instagram-only, Facebook-only)
   - Device splits
   - Creative variations (from graduation framework)
3. **V phase again:** Take H scaling winners and apply vertical scaling on those
4. **Repeat:** Can go H again from new V winners

**Manual bid rule within VHV:** "Max manual bid or lowest cost bid: 3x my target CPA, 2x, and also the same CPA."

**Example with $30 CPA target:** Set one ad set at $90, one at $60, one at $30 manual bid.

**Kill rule:** "If by 1-3pm I should have generated X sales and ROAS should have been Y -- if I'm not seeing this, pause them. Give them a chance tomorrow to restart."

**"Don't feel attached" principle:** "Just because your winning ad set up here was the one that started it all off, it doesn't matter if it stops performing -- pause it."

**Why VHV fails (diagnostic checklist):**
1. Unproven creatives thrown into duplicates (test in PPE first)
2. Ad sets clashing in auction (check delivery insights, <30% overlap)
3. Audience too small after cuts (website conversion needs large audiences)
4. Over-segmentation (Instagram-only + mobile-only + $90 bid = tiny audience, saturates fast)

### 3D. THE 4-STEP SCALING FORMULA

**Depesh's master scaling sequence:**
1. **Graduation Framework** -- Prove audience + creative delivers user value
2. **V Scaling** -- "If you can't get V scaling to work, you're not going to get any other kind of scaling to work." Auto bid first, 20% increments
3. **Ad Sniper Strategy** -- Optimize ads, cut underperformers. Best practice: 3-4 ads per ad set, trim against ad set average
4. **H Scaling** -- Duplicate winners, test bid levels, placements, devices. Then apply V scaling within H scaling winners

**Key insight:** "It's about bringing all those elements together." V scaling within H scaling, Ad Sniper continuously running.

---

## 4. BOSS THE AUCTION WITH MANUAL BID (Framework)

**Auction construct:** Advertiser Bid x Estimated Action Rates x User Value

**Depesh's philosophy:** "User value is the number one goal for you as a marketer and then figuring out how you can reach them most efficiently."

**CPA formula (only 2 inputs):** Cost Per Click x (1/Conversion Rate) = CPA. "Only two metrics influence cost per acquisition: your cost per click and your conversion rate."

**Pre-requisite rule:** "I absolutely recommend you don't use manual bid until your click-through rates and conversion rate are in a good place." Auto bid first, then manual.

**Manual bid testing framework:**
- Start with 1x, 2x, and 3x your CPA target as lowest cost cap
- E.g., $30 CPA target: test $30, $60, $90 manual bid
- Can test in between: $30, $40, $50, $60, $70, $80, $90
- Use H scaling (duplicate ad sets) to test bid levels simultaneously

**Two bid strategies:**
1. **Lowest Cost Cap** -- "Don't bid higher than my CPA target." Limits reach but maximizes profitability. Risk: misses high-value users who'd cost $35 but convert at much higher rate
2. **Target Cost** -- "Average out my CPA." Allows Facebook to go higher on some, lower on others. Better for scaling above 4 figures/day per ad set

**Target cost insight:** "If your CPA target is $30, a $45 or even $50 target cost will be more profitable for you if your conversion rate and click-through rate are doing really, really well."

**When to use which:**
- **Lowest cost cap:** When profitability > growth. Will cause under-delivery some days
- **Target cost:** When scaling spend above $1,000/day per ad set. Gives more reach

**Real example:** $30 CPA target. Tested 30, 50, 75, 90 max bid. The 3x ($90) performed best. Scaled from $280/day to $1,500/day.

**3x rule:** "If you go for three times a target, generally that's the best biting point."

---

## 5. ADS SNIPER STRATEGY (Optimization Methodology)

**Purpose:** Drill down from campaign > ad set > ad level to identify what to pause, scale, or iterate.

**Time window heuristic:** "Generally I want to be looking at the last 3 days. Three days gives a good even view of what's going on." For big-spend accounts: 1-2 days may suffice.

**Statistical validity threshold:** "Around 5,000 impressions to give me confidence these numbers are legit." Relevant score doesn't populate until 500 impressions.

**Analysis process:**
1. Sort by amount spent (highest first -- these are priorities)
2. Compare each ad set's CPA to campaign average
3. For overperforming ad sets: push growth (scale budget)
4. For underperforming ad sets: drill into ads, compare ad-level metrics to ad set average
5. Pause ads below average, let winners take more budget
6. Iterate paused ads (tweak copy, test what made winners work)

**"Snipe both ways" principle:** "It's not just about sniping ads that are not doing well. It's also sniping ads that are doing well and pushing for that performance." If one ad in a good ad set is at 56p CPL while average is 89p -- pause the others and push the winner.

**CPC vs CVR diagnostic:** If an ad has lower CPC but higher CPL than another ad -- the issue is conversion rate, not traffic quality. "It comes down to conversion rate and how many people are converting once they click through."

**Negative comments rule:** "When you see performance dropping at the ad level for an ad that seemingly performed okay -- quite often it comes down to negative comments." HIDE comments (don't delete -- deletion notifies the commenter who'll likely post worse). "It's graffiti. You need to take that graffiti out of your ad."

**Filters and Reports workflow:**
- Use naming conventions (cold/warm/hot + WC/PPE/VV) to enable fast filtering
- "Had Delivery" filter first, THEN campaign filters
- Save filter presets for each funnel stage
- Save as reports (includes columns, dates, breakdowns)
- Schedule daily reports as CSV for spreadsheet analysis

**Automation rules (with caveats):**
- Set rule: pause ad when CPA > $30
- Set complementary rule: restart ad if CPA drops (conversions sometimes come later in the day)
- Minimum impressions threshold (5,000) before auto-pause
- **Warning:** "Automations sometimes fall over" -- they can't see that within a great ad set, one ad is performing at half the cost. Manual analysis catches what machines miss.

**Best practice per ad set:** 3 ads, potentially 4-5 if budget supports it.

---

## DEPESH MANDALIA -- KEY VOCABULARY & TERMINOLOGY

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 3N / 3 Nudge | His core creative framework (Stop-Engage-Convert) |
| Pop the ad | Make visual stand out with color contrast |
| Staircase Scaling | 20% budget increments methodically |
| Punishment signal | 10% budget decrease to reset algorithm |
| V Scaling | Vertical (budget increase on same ad set) |
| H Scaling | Horizontal (duplicate ad sets sideways) |
| VHV | His preferred combined strategy |
| Graduation Framework | Audience + creative testing foundation (referenced, not in these files) |
| Oceans, Boats, and Hooks | Audience = ocean, ad sets = boats, ads = hooks |
| Ad Sniper | Drill-down optimization methodology |
| Insurance policy | Original ad set kept untouched during H scaling |
| Creative refresh | Swapping creatives when frequency rises |
| Boss the auction | Controlling manual bid to dominate auction |
| User value | Facebook's estimation of relevance to user |
| 3x rule | Manual bid at 3x target CPA = best biting point |
| Had Delivery | Filter for active ads only |

---

## QUANTITATIVE HEURISTICS SUMMARY

| Metric | Threshold | Context |
|---|---|---|
| V Scaling increment | 20% max per increase | Budget scaling |
| V Scaling frequency | Max 2x per day | When confident |
| V Scaling compound (5 days) | ~300% total increase | 20%/day compound |
| Budget increase cutoff time | No later than 2-3pm | Prevents wasted spend |
| Punishment decrease | 10% | When performance drops |
| Ad set overlap max | 30% | Delivery insights check |
| Manual bid sweet spot | 3x target CPA | Lowest cost cap |
| Manual bid test range | 1x, 2x, 3x CPA | H scaling duplicates |
| Statistical validity | 5,000 impressions | Before trusting metrics |
| Relevance score threshold | 500 impressions | Before score populates |
| Analysis time window | Last 3 days | Standard; 1-2 days for high spend |
| Ads per ad set | 3 (up to 4-5) | Best practice |
| CPA automation rule | Pause above target + restart rule | With 5,000 impression minimum |
| Target cost vs lowest cost | Use target cost above $1,000/day per ad set | Scaling threshold |

## L4 FRAMEWORKS

- **FW-DM-301:** "FAATT Framework â€” Facebook Ads Attribution & Tracking Trinity. Two interconnected trinities: (1) Tracking Trinity to enhance data quality going INTO the ad platform, (2) Attribution Trinity to enhance reporting coming OUT of the platform. Together they enable better decision-making." | domain: tracking-attribution-master | source: 1-Introduction_to_The_FAATT_Framework.pdf

- **FW-DM-302:** "Tracking Trinity â€” Three data sources feeding the ad platform: (1) Browser Pixel (client-side, suffering most from iOS14), (2) Server Pixel/CAPI (server-side, bypasses browser blocks but still 7-day window), (3) Offline Conversions (raw sales data, bypasses both pixel limitations, 28-day attribution). All three together give Facebook maximum data for optimization." | domain: tracking-architecture | source: 2-Tracking_Trinity_Explained.mp4

- **FW-DM-303:** "Attribution Trinity â€” Three data sources for decision-making: (1) Facebook Ads Manager (narrow, self-serving â€” only FB data, click-based), (2) Google Analytics (cross-channel, last-click, most unbiased), (3) Shopify/Backend (100% of sales data, source of truth for revenue). Compare all three to triangulate reality." | domain: attribution-architecture | source: 6-1-Attribution_Trinity_Explained.mp4

- **FW-DM-304:** "FAATT Campaign Breakdown Report â€” Custom GA report mirroring Ads Manager structure: Campaign > Ad Set > Ad. Uses UTM parameters: utm_campaign={{campaign.name}}, utm_content={{adset.name}}, utm_term={{ad.name}}. Filter: source/medium = facebook/cpc. Enables drill-down analysis matching FB hierarchy." | domain: ga-reporting | source: 7-1-GA_FAATT_Campaign_Breakdown.mp4

- **FW-DM-305:** "Offline Conversions Benefits Stack: (1) Capture sales MISSED by browser and server pixels, (2) See breakdown data (gender, age, country, placement, device), (3) Capture data outside 7-day pixel tracking period (up to 28 days), (4) See iOS14.x+ sales not captured by pixel, (5) Create custom audiences from offline data, (6) Create lookalike audiences from offline data." | domain: offline-conversions | source: 5-Offline_Conversions.pdf

- **FW-DM-306:** "Full Funnel Structure â€” Cold (never engaged) > Warm (engaged but not clicked â€” fans, email list, video viewers, page engagers) > Hot (visited site â€” product page, cart, blog) > Existing Customers. Each level excludes all levels below it." | domain: funnel-architecture | source: 11-2-Correct_Funnel_Audience_Exclusion.mp4

- **FW-DM-307:** "iOS14 Bypass Architecture â€” On-Facebook actions NOT affected by Apple changes: video views, instant experiences, Facebook Shop interactions, page/post engagement, lead forms, Facebook login. Route: run lead forms on FB > push to email > phone calls > track via offline conversions. Entire chain bypasses Apple's restrictions." | domain: ios14-bypass | source: 11-3-Bypassing_iOS14_Limitations.mp4

- **FW-DM-308:** "Post-iOS14 Audience Exclusion Fix â€” Pre-iOS14: excluding View Content also excluded all deeper funnel events by default. Post-iOS14: must EXPLICITLY exclude EVERY pixel event separately (View Content + Add to Cart + Initiate Checkout + Payment Info + Purchase). Also for retargeting: must INCLUDE every pixel event (Add to Cart + Initiate Checkout + Payment Info, minus Purchases)." | domain: audience-architecture | source: 11-2-Correct_Funnel_Audience_Exclusion.mp4

- **FW-DM-309:** "5 Attribution Models Framework (from GA): (1) Last Click â€” who scored the goal, standard reporting model, (2) First Click â€” where awareness starts, useful for top-of-funnel analysis, (3) Linear â€” equal credit to all touchpoints, best for understanding the daisy chain, not for reporting, (4) Position-Based â€” 50/50 split between first and last touch, (5) Time Decay â€” most credit to last touch, decreasing backwards." | domain: attribution-models | source: 11-1-Attribution_Models_Explained.mp4

- **FW-DM-310:** "AEM Priority Framework (Ecommerce) â€” 8 slots available. Value optimization on Purchase takes 4 slots. Recommended priority: (1) Purchase [value opt], (2) Add Payment Info, (3) Initiate Checkout, (4) Add to Cart, (5) View Content. If no value optimization, all 8 slots available for events." | domain: aem-setup | source: 4-2-CAPI_AEM_Setup.mp4

- **FW-DM-311:** "FAATT UTM Tracking Structure â€” utm_source=facebook, utm_medium=cpc (or 'ads'), utm_campaign={{campaign.name}}, utm_content={{adset.name}}, utm_term={{ad.name}}. The curly brackets are Facebook dynamic variables. Must match exactly in GA filters." | domain: utm-architecture | source: 7-4-GA_UTM_Setup.mp4

- **FW-DM-312:** "Zapier Offline Conversion Automation â€” Trigger: New Shopify order > Action 1: Send Facebook CAPI event (purchase, with order ID for dedup, billing data for matching) > Action 2: Send Facebook Offline Conversion (same data, different API endpoint).
