## L5 METHODOLOGIES

- **MET-DM-301:** "FAATT Implementation Checklist â€” Step 1: Install/verify Browser Pixel (schema, diagnostics). Step 2: Install/verify Server Pixel CAPI (events manager, deduplication, match quality). Step 3: Set up Offline Conversions (create event set, upload 90 days history, automate daily). Step 4: Set up GA UTM tracking (copy FAATT tracking string, apply to all ads). Step 5: Import FAATT Campaign Breakdown report in GA. Step 6: Configure Shopify reporting (add UTM columns, filter by source). Step 7: Analyze using Attribution Trinity (compare FB + GA + Shopify data)." | domain: implementation | source: 10-Summary_Recap.mp4

- **MET-DM-302:** "Offline Conversion Setup Process â€” (1) Go to Events Manager > Connect data sources > Offline > Get started. (2) Name your offline event set. (3) Attach to ad accounts. (4) Turn on auto tracking. (5) For first upload: get 90 days of historical data manually via CSV. (6) Include: email, phone, timestamp, purchase value, currency, customer ID. (7) Automate via Zapier/Integromat/API for daily uploads. (8) Verify match rates in Events Manager." | domain: offline-conversion-setup | source: 5-1-Offline_Conversions_Intro_Setup.mp4

- **MET-DM-303:** "CAPI Verification Process â€” (1) Go to Events Manager > select pixel > Overview. (2) Check Conversion API is active. (3) Verify events show both browser and server sources. (4) Check Event Match Quality (target: 7-8 for purchase). (5) Check deduplication rate (89%+ is ideal). (6) Review if any events are NOT being sent by server. (7) Check data freshness (real-time for low volume, hourly for high). (8) Verify domain and set up AEM." | domain: capi-verification | source: 4-1-CAPI_Events_Manager.mp4

- **MET-DM-304:** "Pixel Diagnostics Weekly Check â€” (1) Go to Events Manager > Pixel > Diagnostics. (2) Check for violations (PII in URLs is critical â€” can lead to account ban). (3) Look for plain-text email/phone in URL parameters. (4) Check deduplication issues (same event ID received â€” can ignore). (5) Fix third-party app PII leaks. (6) Do NOT ignore PII violations. (7) Frequency: at least weekly." | domain: pixel-maintenance | source: 3-2-Browser_Pixel_Diagnostics.mp4

- **MET-DM-305:** "Offline Conversion Data Analysis Process â€” (1) In Ads Manager, add columns: Website Purchase ROAS + Purchase ROAS (pixel + offline). (2) Compare the two numbers side by side. (3) Break down by: country, placement, platform/device. (4) Look for iPhone dominance in offline data (iOS14 signal). (5) Check at campaign, ad set, AND ad level. (6) Use as directional indicator, not absolute reporting. (7) Cross-reference with GA data for validation." | domain: offline-analysis | source: 5-2-Using_Offline_Conversion_Data.mp4

- **MET-DM-306:** "GA Assisted Conversions Analysis â€” (1) Go to Conversions > Multi-Channel Funnels > Assisted Conversions. (2) Select Source/Medium dimension. (3) Find Facebook/CPC row. (4) Compare Last Click revenue vs Assisted revenue. (5) Check the assisted/last ratio (higher = more contribution beyond last click). (6) Drill down by campaign (campaign), ad set (content), ad (keyword). (7) Identify campaigns that appear unprofitable on last-click but contribute heavily as assists." | domain: assisted-conversion-analysis | source: 8-2-GA_Assisted_Conversions.mp4

- **MET-DM-307:** "GA Top Conversion Paths Analysis â€” (1) Go to Conversions > Multi-Channel Funnels > Top Conversion Paths. (2) Select Source/Medium or Campaign path. (3) Add Facebook-only segment (source contains facebook, medium contains cpc). (4) Set minimum 2 path interactions. (5) Identify common daisy chains (e.g., Cold > Warm > Convert). (6) Look for top-of-funnel campaigns that INITIATE journeys completed by bottom-of-funnel. (7) Use to justify top-of-funnel spend that appears unprofitable in last-click." | domain: conversion-path-analysis | source: 8-3-GA_Top_Conversion_Paths.mp4

- **MET-DM-308:** "GA Custom Segments for Funnel Analysis â€” (1) Create segment per funnel level: Cold (campaign contains 'cold'), Warm (campaign contains 'warm'), Hot (campaign contains 'hot'). (2) Set source=facebook, medium=cpc/ads. (3) Choose 'users' not 'sessions' for reach-like view (sessions = frequency-like). (4) Apply multiple segments simultaneously for comparison. (5) Overlay on ecommerce reports to see revenue by funnel stage over time." | domain: ga-segmentation | source: 8-5-GA_Custom_Segments.mp4

- **MET-DM-309:** "Post-iOS14 Audience Exclusion Setup â€” (1) For Cold: exclude View Content + Add to Cart + Initiate Checkout + Payment Info + Purchase + Warm engagers + Existing customers. (2) For Warm: exclude ALL site visit events + Existing customers. (3) For Hot retargeting: INCLUDE Add to Cart + Initiate Checkout + Payment Info (not just Add to Cart alone). (4) For all exclusions: use email lists PLUS pixel audiences (pixel alone is unreliable). - **MET-DM-310:** "Zapier Offline Conversion Automation Setup â€” (1) Import pre-built Zap template. (2) Trigger: New Paid Order in Shopify. (3) Action 1 (CAPI): Send purchase event with order ID (dedup), event time, billing email/phone/address, customer ID as external ID. (4) Action 2 (Offline): Send purchase with value, currency, order ID, item number. (5) Authorize Zapier in Events Manager > Settings > Connected Partners. (6) For enhanced matching: extract FBC click ID from landing page URL (requires dev). (7) Always use billing address, NOT shipping. (8) Add gender/DOB if available for breakdown data." | domain: automation-setup | source: 9-2-Automating_Shopify_to_Offline_Conversions.mp4

- **MET-DM-311:** "GA Product Performance Analysis â€” (1) Go to Conversions > Ecommerce > Product Performance. (2) Add secondary dimension: Campaign or Content (ad set) or Keyword (ad). (3) Filter: source/medium contains facebook/cpc. (4) Identify which products are actually being purchased per campaign. (5) Look for mismatches between advertised product and purchased product. (6) If audience buys only dresses but you show shirts, switch creative to dresses. (7) Move top-performing products from retargeting to top-of-funnel ads." | domain: product-attribution | source: 8-1-GA_Product_Ads_Analysis.mp4

- **MET-DM-312:** "Shopify UTM Analysis Process â€” (1) Go to Analytics > Sales attributed to marketing. (2) Edit columns: add Campaign, Content (ad sets), Terms (ads). (3) Manage filters: UTM medium = cpc/ads AND UTM source = facebook. (4) Add Product Title or Product Type column. (5) Cross-reference: which campaigns drive which product purchases. (6) Compare against Ads Manager data. (7) Use for creative optimization: match ad content to what audience actually buys." | domain: shopify-analysis | source: 9-1-Shopify_Product_Ads_Analysis.mp4

- **MET-DM-313:** "EROAS Calculation Method â€” (1) Sum ALL conversions/revenue across ALL channels for a period (7 days recommended). (2) Sum ALL paid marketing spend across ALL channels for same period. (3) EROAS = Total Revenue / Total Paid Spend. (4) ECPA = Total Paid Spend / Total Conversions. (5) Use as health check: is the overall machine profitable? (6) Not for campaign-level optimization â€” for macro business decisions. (7) Track over time as a trend, not absolute." | domain: eroas-methodology | source: 11-1-Attribution_Models_Explained.mp4

- FIL-DM-418: "Discounting from day one trains your customers to only buy on discount. If you start with discounting, you end up a discount retailer like DFS." | domain: pricing-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_03-From_AC-4_To_Advertising_Strategy_.md
- FIL-DM-419: "A perfect customer is one that doesn't focus on price because they love the value, converts highly, tells friends and family, and buys again." | domain: customer-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_4_Key_Pillars_of_Advertising.md
- FIL-DM-420: "Brands create experiences that people enjoy. That's where true growth comes from â€” lifetime value and customer loyalty." â€” Growth > scaling | domain: brand-philosophy | source: transcription_mp4_01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md

## L2 MENTAL-MODELS (Thinking Frameworks)

- MM-DM-401: "AC-4 (Advertising Core 4)" â€” The four pillars that determine advertising success: Product, Audience, Offer, Funnel. Grade each 1-5. Total score out of 20 determines readiness to scale | domain: advertising-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_4_Key_Pillars_of_Advertising.md
- MM-DM-402: "BPM = Brand Driven Performance Marketing" â€” Merge brand marketing (understanding the consumer) with performance marketing (understanding the ad platform) through creative messaging | domain: marketing-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_The_BPM_Method_.md
- MM-DM-403: "Maslow's Hierarchy applied to Advertising" â€” Products sit at different levels of the hierarchy. The higher on Maslow's you pitch, the more emotional and harder to rationalize price, but higher conversion potential | domain: consumer-psychology | source: transcription_mp4_02-Human_Needs___Desires_.md
- MM-DM-404: "Blue Ocean vs Red Ocean for product positioning" â€” Carve out a niche within a competitive market to create your own blue ocean | domain: product-positioning | source: transcription_mp4_01-Is_Your_Product-Service_Ads_Ready.md
- MM-DM-405: "Awareness x Intent Matrix (3x3)" â€” Map audiences by awareness level (low/med/high) and intent level (low/med/high) to determine communication strategy and campaign structure | domain: audience-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_02-Into_The_Mind_of_Your_Prospects_.md
- MM-DM-406: "Facebook Algorithm Triangle" â€” Advertiser Bid x Estimated Action Rate + User Value = Total Auction Value. All three must be optimized | domain: meta-algorithm | source: transcription_mp4_02-The_Facebook_Algorithm_UNLEASHED_.md
- MM-DM-407: "The Three Players Model" â€” User, Advertiser, Ad Platform. If you make the user happy, the platform is also happy and rewards you with lower costs | domain: meta-ads-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_02-Compulsion_Offers_That_Attract__Convince___Convert_.md
- MM-DM-408: "Discount Bidding Mechanism" â€” Facebook gives new ads an artificial boost within the first ~500 impressions. Use this to pre-test ads cheaply before moving winners to main campaigns | domain: testing-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- MM-DM-409: "Not all users are created equal in the auction" â€” A clicky user costs less but never buys. A buyer-type user costs more but converts. Campaign objective determines which user type you get | domain: meta-auction | source: transcription_mp4_02-The_Facebook_Algorithm_UNLEASHED_.md
- MM-DM-410: "Hunters, Trackers, and Explorers" â€” Three types of site visitors. Only a small percentage (2-6%) are hunters ready to buy now. 20-30% are trackers in research mode. The rest are explorers | domain: conversion-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_Customer_Experience_Framework.md
- MM-DM-411: "The Value Exchange Model" â€” You send the user value (ad/offer), user sends you action (click/buy), Facebook sends you cost. If value > cost of action to user, they convert. Facebook monitors the value you deliver | domain: meta-ads-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_06-The_New___Simplified_Facebook_Ads_Success_Model_.md
- MM-DM-412: "ROAS = AOV / CPA. CPA = CPC / Conversion Rate" â€” Cascading KPI model where each metric influences the others. AOV is easier to influence than CPA | domain: kpi-management | source: transcription_mp4_01-KPIs___Goal_Setting_.md
- MM-DM-413: "Growth vs Scaling" â€” Scaling = spending more money faster (higher risk). Growth = balancing operational stability with revenue growth (sustainable). Most should choose growth over scaling | domain: business-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md
- MM-DM-414: "EROAS (Estimated ROAS)" â€” Use Google Analytics total revenue / Facebook spend as a trend indicator. Facebook typically drives 60-70% of direct-attributed sales but contributes to all other channels too | domain: attribution | source: transcription_mp4_02-eROAS_Calculator_.md
- MM-DM-415: "Functional vs Emotional selling" â€” Functional = rational thinking, can quantify, yes/no decision. Emotional = irrational, cannot put a dollar value on the feeling, converts higher | domain: copywriting-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_03-Storytelling__Visualisation___Emotion.md
- MM-DM-416: "LED Dog Collar Pitch Shift" â€” Example of moving a product from Safety (rational: plastic + battery = $30) to Love & Belonging (emotional: could save your dog's life = priceless) | domain: offer-positioning | source: transcription_mp4_02-Human_Needs___Desires_.md
- MM-DM-417: "V-Scale vs H-Scale" â€” Vertical scaling = increase existing campaign budgets. Horizontal scaling = duplicate campaigns or create new ones. Both have different stability/risk profiles | domain: scaling-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_Campaign_Scaling_Strategies_.md

- HEUR-DM-401: "AC-4 Score 20/20 = perfect (never seen). 15-19 = ready to scale. 10-14 = too early to scale, optimize first. Below 9 = major work needed beyond just ads." | domain: advertising-readiness | source: transcription_mp4_02-Grading_Your_AC-4_.md
- HEUR-DM-402: "Attention to engagement transition must happen within 5-7 seconds. Actual attention capture = 0.5 to 2 seconds." | domain: ad-creative | source: transcription_mp4_02-Compulsion_Offers_That_Attract__Convince___Convert_.md
- HEUR-DM-403: "Lower product value = shorter funnel. A $25 product needs cold + retargeting only. A $2,000+ product needs cold + warm + hot + existing customer funnels." | domain: funnel-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_02-The_4_Funnel_System_Explained.md
- HEUR-DM-404: "For testing, find 3-5 audiences that give a good 2-3 months of test runway before scaling." | domain: audience-testing | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- HEUR-DM-405: "Ads fatigue faster than audiences. Creative shelf life is shorter than audience shelf life." | domain: creative-management | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- HEUR-DM-406: "CPM of $20 at 2% CTR = $1 CPC. If CPM halved to $10, CPC drops to $0.50, ROAS doubles â€” without changing anything else." | domain: kpi-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_01-KPIs___Goal_Setting_.md
- HEUR-DM-407: "Doubling CTR often drops conversion rate. Net impact isn't as high. Lower CPM with maintained CTR/CR is usually better." | domain: meta-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_01-KPIs___Goal_Setting_.md
- HEUR-DM-408: "For e-commerce, site conversion rate typically 1-4%. Info products/digital 5-15%. Webinars anywhere in between." | domain: conversion-benchmarks | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- HEUR-DM-409: "The Punisher Method: Reduce budget by 10% when performance drops during scaling. Give 24-48 hours to recover. This sends a negative signal that kick-starts the algorithm." | domain: budget-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_03-The_Punisher_Method_to_Reset_Performance_.md
- HEUR-DM-410: "V-Scale: Increase budget 20% at a time. Compound effect: $500 to $600 to $720 to $864 to $1,037 â€” can double spend in a single day with CBO." | domain: scaling-tactics | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_Campaign_Scaling_Strategies_.md
- HEUR-DM-411: "If someone clicks your ad and bounces back to Facebook fast, it's a massive negative signal. Never delay pixel fire â€” Facebook measures time between click and pixel fire." | domain: pixel-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_02-The_Facebook_Algorithm_UNLEASHED_.md
- HEUR-DM-412: "For ad sniping: need at least 1 week of spend, minimum 10-15 conversions (ideally 30-50) before making optimization decisions." | domain: optimization-thresholds | source: transcription_mp4_04-The_Ads_Sniper_Method_to_Optimise_Spend_.md
- HEUR-DM-413: "LTV calculation: use last 12 months as rolling baseline. Total revenue / total unique customers = LTV per customer." | domain: lifetime-value | source: transcription_mp4_01-KPIs___Goal_Setting_.md
- HEUR-DM-414: "When scaling up high (200K/day spend), ads had 2-3 day shelf life. Had to refresh creatives 1-2x per week." | domain: creative-fatigue | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- HEUR-DM-415: "Allow 3 months from hire date to fully productive for any new role (customer service, media manager, account manager)." | domain: team-scaling | source: transcription_mp4_01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md
- HEUR-DM-416: "Test with as few impressions as possible in graduation testing (~500 impressions) to leverage discount bidding, then move winners to main campaigns." | domain: testing-efficiency | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- HEUR-DM-417: "E-commerce store case study: AC-4 scored 17/20. Biggest lever was the offer. Scaled from $500/day to $30-35K/day at high profitability. Took ski apparel brand to $16M in 5 months." | domain: scaling-benchmarks | source: transcription_mp4_02-Grading_Your_AC-4_.md
- HEUR-DM-418: "Cold funnel ROI of 1x (break-even) is acceptable if hot/retargeting campaigns convert at 4-5x ROAS, giving overall 3-3.5x ROAS." | domain: funnel-economics | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- HEUR-DM-419: "Facebook typically shows only 60-70% of total sales attributable. The other 30-40% comes through email, organic, branded search â€” all initiated by Facebook." | domain: attribution-benchmarks | source: transcription_mp4_02-eROAS_Calculator_.md
- HEUR-DM-420: "Spend 30 minutes to 1 hour on strategy (avatar, core 4, offer) before spending any money on ads. This is where 80-90% of success comes from." | domain: strategy-first | source: transcription_mp4_06-The_New___Simplified_Facebook_Ads_Success_Model_.md
- HEUR-DM-421: "Planned growth timeline: Day 0 launch at $100/day, Month 1 to $1,000/day, Month 3 to $5,000/day, Month 6 to $20,000/day â€” building systems at each stage." | domain: growth-pacing | source: transcription_mp4_01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md
- HEUR-DM-422: "Info product CPMs typically $30-50. E-commerce CPMs generally lower. Info product space needs stronger AOV work because CPA reduction is harder." | domain: cpm-benchmarks | source: transcription_mp4_01-AOV_Optimisation_Methods_.md

## L4 FRAMEWORKS (Structured Methodologies)

- FW-DM-401: "The AC-4 Framework (Advertising Core 4)" â€” Product (solution/opportunity score 1-5) + Audience (ideal customer identification 1-5) + Offer (proposition/hook 1-5) + Funnel (conversion ability 1-5) = Total /20. Action plan: identify biggest lever, prioritize the one thing for highest leverage, work sequentially | domain: advertising-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_4_Key_Pillars_of_Advertising.md + transcription_mp4_02-Grading_Your_AC-4_.md
- FW-DM-402: "The 5W Avatar System" â€” WHO am I selling to? WHAT is the product (simple, no fluff)? WOW (emotional/intangible benefits â€” cannot be measured, state change, ego, pain/solution). WHY (functional benefits â€” shipping, fit, comfort, guarantee). WHY NOT (objections/barriers to buying). Each WOW line = different angle to test. Each WHY NOT = retargeting ad angle | domain: avatar-building | source: transcription_mp4_03-Building_Your_5W_Avatar_.md
- FW-DM-403: "The 4 Funnel System" â€” Cold (never heard of you, prospects) + Warm (engaged but not clicked â€” video viewers, page fans, email list) + Hot (clicked to site but not purchased) + Existing (past customers). Different creatives, objectives, and strategies per level | domain: funnel-architecture | source: transcription_mp4_02-The_4_Funnel_System_Explained.md
- FW-DM-404: "The 3N Ads Formula (3 Nudges)" â€” N1: Stop the Scroll (thumb-stopping content, visual disruption). N2: Engage (keep them, hook them in first 5 seconds, build interest). - FW-DM-405: "AIDA Model for Ad Copy" â€” Attention (big bold statement, first 2 lines are everything). Interest (big hook, bridge story from problem to solution). Desire (reach for outcomes, improvement, state change). Action (tell them exactly what to do â€” click here, download, sign up) | domain: copywriting | source: transcription_mp4_03-Building_Your_5W_Avatar_.md + transcription_mp4_03-Storytelling__Visualisation___Emotion.md
- FW-DM-406: "The Graduation Framework (3 Phases)" â€” Phase 1: Find Winning Audiences (test 3-5 audiences with proven ads, minimal spend). Phase 2: Find Winning Ads (test new ads against established audiences, conveyor belt of creative). Phase 3: Stabilize/Optimize/Scale (move proven ads to main campaigns, maintain stability) | domain: testing-framework | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- FW-DM-407: "STAR Testing Method" â€” Setup (prioritize test backlog, build test). Test (run it). Analyze (why did it work or not, attach data). Repeat (take learnings into next cycle). Kanban board: Ideas > Prioritized > Work In Progress > Live > On Hold > Analysis > Winner/Loser | domain: testing-process | source: transcription_mp4_13-STAR_Testing_Method_.md
- FW-DM-408: "The Punisher Method" â€” When campaign performance drops during scaling: reduce budget by 10%, wait 24-48 hours for algorithm reset. With CBO: drill to ad level first, pause underperformers. Sends "unhappy" signal to Facebook | domain: optimization | source: transcription_mp4_03-The_Punisher_Method_to_Reset_Performance_.md
- FW-DM-409: "The Ad Sniper Method" â€” Drill from campaign average ROAS > ad set level > ad level. Find ads performing below average. Pause underperformers. Remaining better-performing ads pull up the average. Daily task. Automate with rules once mastered manually | domain: optimization | source: transcription_mp4_04-The_Ads_Sniper_Method_to_Optimise_Spend_.md
- FW-DM-410: "The Three Rs of Marketing" â€” Relationship (I'm here for you â€” matchmaking ads to audiences via grad testing). Rapport (I understand you â€” building goals through funnel). Relevance (I know what you need â€” delivering right ad at right time via retargeting sequence) | domain: marketing-strategy | source: transcription_mp4_02-Into_The_Mind_of_Your_Prospects_.md
- FW-DM-411: "Offer Compulsion Framework" â€” Attention (visual + first line copy, sub-7 seconds) > Engagement (keep them, curiosity/hook) > Action (click/watch/sign up). Works on any platform. Focus on user first, not platform formats | domain: offer-creation | source: transcription_mp4_02-Compulsion_Offers_That_Attract__Convince___Convert_.md
- FW-DM-412: "The Customer Experience Framework" â€” Analysis (Analytics, Surveys/NPS, Session Capture, Personas, CRM data) > Landing Page Optimization > Campaign Planning > Creative Testing > Merchandising > Behavioral Targeting > Split Testing > Develop Ideas. Deep CRO process built since 2005 | domain: conversion-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_01-The_Customer_Experience_Framework.md
- FW-DM-413: "V-Scale and H-Scale Strategy Matrix" â€” V-Scale: 20% budget increases on existing campaigns (compound effect). H-Scale: Duplicate campaigns for new countries/segments/audiences. - FW-DM-414: "EROAS Attribution Framework" â€” Merge Google Analytics (total sessions, transactions, revenue) with Facebook spend data. Calculate estimated ROAS = total GA revenue / FB spend. ## L5 METHODOLOGIES (Step-by-Step Processes)

- MET-DM-401: "BPM Method Complete Process" â€” Step 1: AC-4 grading (score product/audience/offer/funnel). Step 2: Identify biggest lever. Step 3: Build 5W Avatar. Step 4: Create ad copy using AIDA. Step 5: Build 4 Funnel System (cold/warm/hot/existing). Step 6: Set KPIs & goal targets. Step 7: Set up attribution tracking (eROAS). Step 8: Run Graduation Testing (Phase 1: audiences, Phase 2: ads, Phase 3: stability). Step 9: Stabilize & Optimize. Step 10: Scale via V-Scale/H-Scale | domain: complete-system | source: multiple files across both sections
- MET-DM-402: "5W Avatar Construction Process" â€” Step 1: Define WHAT (product, simple description). Step 2: Define WHO (specific avatar, demographics + psychographics). Step 3: List WOW factors (emotional benefits, one per line, each = test angle). Step 4: List WHY factors (functional benefits). Step 5: List WHY NOT factors (objections/barriers). Step 6: Write AIDA ad copy for each WOW angle. Step 7: Create variants (5 headlines x different visuals x body copy variants = 15-25 ads per angle) | domain: avatar-methodology | source: transcription_mp4_03-Building_Your_5W_Avatar_.md
- MET-DM-403: "Graduation Testing Process" â€” Phase 1 (Find Winning Audiences): Pick 3-5 audiences, use proven ad(s), test at low spend, measure within ~500 impressions via discount bidding. Phase 2 (Find Winning Ads): Take winning audiences, test new ads continuously, build a conveyor belt of creative. Phase 3 (Stabilize): Move proven ad+audience pairs to main prospecting campaigns, never drop untested ads into scaling campaigns | domain: testing-methodology | source: transcription_mp4_01-_Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework_.md
- MET-DM-404: "AOV Optimization Process" â€” Step 1: Price testing (split test new price to cold audience only, offer discount to retargeting to offset). Step 2: Bundles (buy 2 get 10% off, buy 3 get 15% off). Step 3: Upsells/cross-sells. Step 4: One-time offers in funnel. Step 5: Post-purchase email sequences for repeat purchase. If AOV doubles from $40 to $80, ROAS doubles without touching CPA | domain: aov-optimization | source: transcription_mp4_01-AOV_Optimisation_Methods_.md
- MET-DM-405: "Ad Copy Construction Process (Story Selling)" â€” Step 1: Pick one emotional angle from 5W Avatar WOW list. Step 2: Write functional version (product features + discount). Step 3: Write emotional version (story that evokes visualization and feeling). Step 4: Write pure experience version (no product mention, sell the outcome). Step 5: Test all three. Step 6: For winners, create video version with voiceover. Step 7: Merge emotional hook + functional benefits in retargeting ads | domain: copywriting-methodology | source: transcription_mp4_03-Storytelling__Visualisation___Emotion.md
- MET-DM-406: "Stabilization & Optimization Process" â€” Step 1: Confirm stable ROAS/CPA for 5-7 days minimum. Step 2: Optimize AOV first (pricing, bundles, upsells â€” most controllable). Step 3: Optimize conversion rate (page speed, micro-conversions, funnel drop-off analysis). Step 4: Reduce CPM (page quality, ad quality scores, auction insights analysis). Step 5: Apply Ad Sniper method daily (trim underperforming ads). Step 6: Use Punisher Method if performance drops during optimization. Step 7: Only scale once stable at optimized KPIs | domain: optimization-methodology | source: transcription_mp4_01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- MET-DM-407: "Scaling Pre-Flight Checklist" â€” Before scaling, verify: (1) Creative pipeline ready for refresh 1-2x/week. (2) Website load-tested for traffic spikes. (3) Customer service team aware and staffed. (4) Fulfillment capacity confirmed. (5) Payment processor informed of spend increase. (6) Credit card limits checked. (7) Pixel maturity sufficient (weeks of good data). (8) Audience size large enough for spend level. (9) Cash liquidity for ad payments | domain: scaling-methodology | source: transcription_mp4_01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md
- MET-DM-408: "KPI Cascading Model Setup" â€” Step 1: Define target ROAS. Step 2: Set AOV (average selling price x items per cart). Step 3: Calculate required CPA (AOV / target ROAS). Step 4: Model CPC needed (CPA x conversion rate). Step 5: Check if achievable CPM exists (CPC x CTR x 1000). Step 6: Use the spreadsheet calculator to model scenarios. Step 7: Identify which metric to optimize first based on control level | domain: kpi-planning | source: transcription_mp4_01-KPIs___Goal_Setting_.md
- MET-DM-409: "4 Funnel System Setup for Ecom" â€” Cold: interests/lookalikes/broad, video + static creative showcasing product benefits, optimize for purchase or ATC. Warm: video viewers (75%+), page engagers, email list not clicked; use deeper content, influencers, stylists. Hot: site visitors not purchased, cart abandoners; use testimonials, reminder ads, DPA, barrier-breaking copy. Existing: purchase custom audiences; use loyalty offers, new product launches, repurchase incentives | domain: funnel-setup | source: transcription_mp4_02-Grading_Your_AC-4_.md + transcription_mp4_02-The_4_Funnel_System_Explained.md
- MET-DM-410: "Facebook Attribution Pause Test" â€” Pause all Facebook ads for 1 full week. Track impact on every other channel (email, SEO, paid search, affiliates, organic). When all channels drop, it proves Facebook was contributing to their volume. Calculate true Facebook contribution = direct FB attributed + channel lift | domain: attribution-methodology | source: transcription_mp4_02-eROAS_Calculator_.md

## MODULE 02: BUILD YOUR OFFER

### Core Philosophy
- **The offer is NOT about discounts or promotion.** The offer is what bridges your product/service with your audience and pushes them into your funnel to convert.
- Success comes from the offer, not from the platform. Most people dive straight into Ads Manager and fail because they skip offer construction.
- "Spend a little bit of time doing the planning and the research and building your campaign -- it will give you the stability, the profitability and the scalability that you're looking for."

### Unique Terminology
- **Offer Bridge** -- the connection between product/service and audience that creates funnel entry

---

## MODULE 12: ADS MASTERY USING CONVERSION PSYCHOLOGY

### Framework: The 3N Ads Formula (3-Nudge Ads Formula)
The core ad creation framework. "N" = Nudge. Based on behavioral economics concept of influencing choice without coercion.

**Definition of Nudge:** "Influencing the choice that someone has, but in a way that does not force them to do so. The intervention must be easy and true to avoid."

**Three Nudges:**
1. **N1: Stop the Scroll** -- Get them to stop scrolling (thumb-stopping content)
2. **N2: Engage** -- Capture and retain their attention
3. **N3: Convert** -- Move them to take action

## MODULE 12: ADS MASTERY USING CONVERSION PSYCHOLOGY

### Framework: The 3N Ads Formula (3-Nudge Ads Formula)
The core ad creation framework. "N" = Nudge. Based on behavioral economics concept of influencing choice without coercion.

**Definition of Nudge:** "Influencing the choice that someone has, but in a way that does not force them to do so. The intervention must be easy and true to avoid."

**Three Nudges:**
1. **N1: Stop the Scroll** -- Get them to stop scrolling (thumb-stopping content)
2. **N2: Engage** -- Capture and retain their attention
3. **N3: Convert** -- Move them to take action

### Hierarchy of Importance
"Get your visual absolutely right FIRST, then get them to stop scrolling. If you cannot get them to stop the scroll, the first part of your advertising journey has been a failure. You can forget everything else -- winning the auction, great copy, great call to action, landing page, even a great product."

### N1: Stop the Scroll -- Techniques
1. **Pop the Ad** -- Add color tone to make ads stand out (slightly unnatural colors work because they're unexpected)
2. **Product Color Contrast** -- Use product colors to create visual contrast
3. **Grayscale** -- Use black/white sparingly for different effect
4. **Cropping and Spacing** -- Use empty space to make subject pop (J.Crew example)
5. **Authentic vs Staged** -- Avoid overly staged shots; smiling/happy/relatable people work
6. **Eye Contact** -- People looking at the viewer force attention to their eyes
7. **Thumbnails** -- "The thumbnail is absolutely more important than the video itself because without a strong thumbnail, you're not going to get people to stop the scroll"
8. **Cycle visuals more than copy** -- "In the hierarchy of importance, get your visual absolutely right"

### N2: Engage -- Decision Rule
"You've got literally a few seconds to get them to take note of your ad and then a few seconds, if that, to get them to engage with your content."

**Key Heuristic:** Deliver the payload EARLY. For video, bring the end-frames/proposition to the front. "Delivering that payload as early as possible in that video paid dividends."

**Copy Approaches (test these against each other):**
1. **Spark Curiosity** -- Identify and solve a problem
2. **Be Controversial** -- "Don't sit on the fence. Either be for or against, supportive or destructive." Attack the user to evoke reaction.
3. **Relate/Befriend** -- Softer approach, empathy-first
4. **Customer State Matching** -- Copy should reflect where they are (cold/warm/hot/existing)
5. **Urgency** -- Used sparingly and at the right moment
6. **Long Copy** -- "Don't be afraid of long copy. It absolutely works." But the first few lines matter most. "If you don't get those first few lines right, no one's gonna click your read more."

### Awareness Matrix (4 quadrants)
| Customer Awareness | Product Awareness | Approach |
|---|---|---|
| Aware of solution needed | Aware of product | Price/promotion (hardest to differentiate) |
| Aware of solution needed | NOT aware of product | Product demos, expertise |
| NOT aware of problem | Aware of product type | Convince them of the problem |
| NOT aware of problem | NOT aware of product | Full education needed (hardest) |

### Key Engagement Levers
- **Goals** -- What are they trying to get?
- **Struggles** -- What are their pain points?
- **False Beliefs** -- What do they believe that isn't true?
- **Opportunity Cost** -- What's the cost of inaction?
- **Impulsiveness** -- For low-ticket ($20-30), push impulse with social proof and cool videos

### Maslow's Hierarchy Applied to Ads
- Bottom of pyramid (food/water/sleep) = tangible, less copy needed
- Top of pyramid (self-esteem/confidence/achievement) = intangible, need longer copy, more storytelling, probably email capture funnels
- "As you go up the hierarchy, you end up elongating the content, the funnel, the communication"

### Engagement Formula
- **Appeal to the Emotional State FIRST**, then speak to the Functional State
- "A lot of people go straight to the functional. They miss the emotional out and then wonder why their ad doesn't work."

### Copywriting Structure (Newspaper Pyramid)
- Headline: Fewest words possible (hook)
- Sub-headline: Few more nuggets
- First section: Chunky information
- Main content: Full journey
- Based on John Caples, David Ogilvy principles

### N3: Convert -- Techniques
1. **Tell people what to do** -- Explicitly say "pick yours now," "click the link below"
2. **Social Proof** -- 7,500 likes makes people curious. BUT don't build fake social proof from cheap CPM countries (dirties your page). Instead run page post engagement campaigns.
3. **Clear Next Steps** -- "I'll email you my 49 tips" -- tell them exactly what happens after click
4. **Urgency with Bonus** -- "Act today and receive a bonus growth swipe file"
5. **Call to Action Testing** -- Softer CTAs higher in funnel, direct CTAs for retargeting

### Framework: AIDA Applied to Facebook Ads
- **Attention** -- Big bold statement, reach for problem/opportunity. First 2 lines matter most.
- **Interest** -- Build a bridge story from problem to solution
- **Desire** -- Reach out to outcomes, improve their situation
- **Action** -- Tell them what to do next. Include website URL in copy. Guide them explicitly.

### Framework: 5W Avatar + Ad Copy Construction
Three levels of ad sophistication (demonstrated with $49.99 personalized necklace example):

**Level 1 (Typical/Functional):** Direct product benefits, features, discount. "Buy this product." Leads to rational price evaluation. Yes/no decision.

**Level 2 (Emotional Hook):** Story-based copy that evokes visualization and feeling. "Experience this feeling." Cannot quantify a feeling against $50. Dramatic improvement in engagement and conversion.

**Level 3 (Pure Experience):** Don't even show the product -- sell the experience via video, voiceover, imagery. "$50 feels cheap because you want that experience."

### Depesh's Unique Concept: Story Selling
"Story selling -- it doesn't matter if I'm selling e-com, info product, anything in between -- if I can evoke a feeling, I have more chance of grabbing you into my funnel than if I make you think about something more functional."

### Heuristic: Thoughts vs Feelings
"If you ask me to THINK about price and functional benefits, I can quantify $50 and it's a simple yes or no. If you evoke a FEELING, you're giving me something my brain can't construct a $50 value for."

### Pricing Heuristic from Better Copy
- Better emotional copy = higher conversion rate = lower CPA
- Better emotional copy = ability to increase price (sell on value)
- Both effects compound: CPA drops AND ROAS increases

### Barrier-Breaking Process (from David Ogilvy)
"Write down all of the customer barriers. What are they likely to say no to? How can you break those down? Attack is the best form of defense."

### Merge Strategy
Combine emotional hook copy with functional benefits lower in the ad. Use functional benefits in retargeting ads to break down remaining barriers.

---

## MODULE 24: GROW & SCALE

### Core Distinction: Growth vs Scaling
- **Scaling** = Spending more money on ads and maintaining/improving profitability
- **Growth** = Balancing operational stability with revenue growth

### Growth Timeline Comparison
- **Fast path:** Day 0 ($100) -> Day 15 ($1,000) -> Day 30 ($5,000) -> Day 50 ($20,000) -- HIGH RISK
- **Planned path:** Day 0 ($100) -> Day 30 ($1,000) -> Day 90 ($5,000) -> Day 180 ($20,000) -- SUSTAINABLE

### What Breaks When You Scale (Checklist)
1. Creative fatigue (audiences reached too fast)
2. Instability in ad accounts
3. Website can't handle traffic spikes (load testing needed)
4. Team can't handle customer inquiries (5 emails/day -> 50+)
5. Media buyer stress (200K/day = "most stressful media buying I've ever done")
6. Fulfillment capacity
7. Customer satisfaction drops
8. Payment provider limits / fraud flags
9. Payment processor flags for revenue spikes
10. Higher refund rates, lower page scores, loss of goodwill

### Scaling Readiness Checklist
- Ad account spend limit raised from default?
- Payment provider can support higher spends?
- Ad account operational for at least 4 weeks?
- Avatar and account build implemented?
- At least 5 days of consistent performance before scaling?
- Website speed optimized?
- Website can handle larger traffic volumes?
- Team aware of new sales/leads entering pipeline?
- Can fulfill product/service at volume?
- Shipping operations can cope with expected volumes?
- Customer services ready for more inquiries?

### Three Scaling Mindsets
1. **The Beginner** -- Never increased spend or failed at it. Use V-Scale exclusively. Learn account behaviors first.
2. **The Controller** -- Some scaling experience. Balance risk/reward. Favor stability. Experiment V-Scale -> Nitro V-Scale -> then H-Scale and M-Scale.
3. **The High Roller** -- Experienced. Prefers fast scaling rewards. Push into M-Scale and H-Scale with V-Scale Nitro.

### Risk-Reward Matrix (2x2)
- **Low Risk (bottom-left):** Slow scale, slow reward. $10->$20->$50/day. Safe starting point.
- **High Reward, Slow Scale (top-left):** Where Depesh likes to take things initially. Controlled growth.
- **High Reward, Fast Scale (top-right):** Ultimate goal. Requires confidence in product, audience, creative, bidding, optimization, landing page, funnel.
- **High Risk, Low Reward (bottom-right):** AVOID. Fast budget increase without controls = waste money.

### Progression Path
Beginner -> Controller -> High Roller (never skip Controller stage)

### Day Trading Analogy
Depesh learned from 6-9 months of forex day trading (2008): "The most important things are knowing your stop losses and when to enter." Applied to Facebook: know when to scale up AND when to cut.

### Heuristic: Hiring Timeline
"For any role, I always give myself three months from point of hire to 100% in control."

### Scaling Decision: Scale vs Grow
- **Scale if:** Seasonal dates, competitive pressure, promotional offers, affiliate marketing. More stressful but higher short-term potential.
- **Grow if:** You can balance operational stability with revenue growth. "That's where brands are created, that's where lifetime value is built and customer loyalty."

---

## MODULE 25: CAMPAIGN SCALING STRATEGIES

### Framework: GT-CBO Method (Graduation Testing Campaign Budget Optimization)
Seven-part scaling system:
1. **Core 4** -- Pillars of growth. Must score 16+ out of 20 to be ready for scaling.
2. **5W Avatar** -- Clearly defined market targeting
3. **Four Funnel System** -- Structure for the journey
4. **CBO Application** -- Campaign Budget Optimization setup
5. **Graduation Testing** -- Research audiences/ads, move winners to prospecting
6. **Stability & Optimization** -- Stable CPM, CPC, conversion rates, AOV
7. **Scale Testing** -- Bump budgets, wait for stability, then bump again. Introduce automated rules.

### Two Fundamental Scaling Directions
- **V-Scale (Vertical)** -- Increase budget on existing campaigns
- **H-Scale (Horizontal)** -- Duplicate campaigns/ad sets or introduce new campaigns

### Heuristic: Data Quality
"The more you spend, the more you're telling Facebook you're happy. The less you spend, the more you're telling Facebook you're unhappy."

---

### Strategy 1: V-Scale (Vertical Scaling)

**Definition:** Moderate budget increases on existing campaigns. Lower risk, more predictable.

**Key Threshold:** Facebook advises budget increases under 40% have less impact on the machine.

**Rules:**
- Start with 20% budget bumps if inexperienced
- Increase budgets between 8-10am ad account timezone
- Too early = bad audience pockets + previous day carryover
- Too late = pacing algorithm overspends trying to spend before midnight
- Check results each morning 8-10am

**V-Scale Decision Rule:**
> If CBO performing consistently well for last 3 days (not including today) AND yesterday's performance is within target -> Increase budget by 30% at 9am.

**Scale Testing Phase:**
- After stability, V-scale at least twice within a 7-10 day period
- E.g., $1,000 -> $1,200 (wait 1-2 days) -> +20-30% again
- Combine with Punisher Method (drop budget 10% if performance drops)
- Combine with Ad Sniper (pause bad performing ads)
- If results maintained = passed scale testing, ready for aggressive scaling

### Heuristic: Budget Increase Timing
- Best: 6am-12pm ad account time
- Good: up to 2-3pm
- Bad: late afternoon/evening (pacing algorithm goes crazy trying to spend remainder)
- Depesh's preference: after 6am for clean break from previous day

### Concept: Ad Account Pulse
"Every ad account has a different pulse. No two accounts will be the same. Even two very similar e-commerce businesses will react in different ways."

---

### Strategy 2: Nitro V-Scale

**Definition:** Vertical scaling at much higher percentage increases (50% to 500%+), multiple times per day.

**Prerequisites:**
- Stability for at least 7 days
- Strong audience size (1M+)
- Strong funnel and backup ads for fatigue
- Core 4 grading 16+ out of 20
- At least 50 conversions per week, 200+ lifetime per ad set
- CRO and auction optimizations already done
- Scale testing already completed

**Execution:**
- Push 50% to 500%+ budget bumps
- Multiple bumps per day (ideally between 6am-12pm)
- Budget increases after 6am (not midnight -- previous day buyers cause instability)
- Real example: $972 -> $2,500 (10am) -> $5,000 (7pm) in one day

**Automated Stop-Loss Rules (Ecommerce):**
1. If spent 3X ideal CPA at ad level with no sales AND cost per Initiate Checkout < 25% of target -> PAUSE AD
2. Up until 2pm: If have a sale AND spent > 3X CPA AND ROAS < 50% of target -> PAUSE AD
3. 2pm-5pm: If have a sale AND spent > 3X CPA AND ROAS < 20% of target -> PAUSE AD
4. 5pm-midnight: If have a sale AND spent > 3X CPA AND ROAS < ideal target -> PAUSE AD

**The Punisher Method:**
- If by 3pm negative ad set performance (ROAS/CPA not on track) -> drop CBO budgets by 10%
- If no recovery within 2 hours -> drop to original budget

**Overnight Reset Rule:**
- At midnight, reset budget to previous day's start budget + 20%
- This prevents Facebook from overspending early morning with inflated budget and no history

**Performance Numbers:**
- "$7M in sales with around $950k spend" using this method with full BPM method in place
- Real case: $972/day -> $5,000/day in 9 hours (Black Friday)

---

### Strategy 3: H-Scale Split (Horizontal Scale - Split)

**Definition:** Pull out profitable segments from existing campaigns into separate campaigns with own budgets.

**The Sharon Paradox** -- Depesh's concept illustrating why simple attribution is wrong:
- Sharon sees Instagram ad (morning) -> watches Instagram story (lunch) -> sees DPA on Facebook (commute) -> buys via Google brand search (evening)
- Facebook attributes to Instagram story. Google Analytics attributes to brand search. Neither is fully correct. The DPA reminder was critical.
- Lesson: Be cautious when splitting placements based on attribution data alone.

**Unique Segment Priority (safe to split):**
1. Age -- People don't change age during the day
2. Gender -- Stable
3. Country/Region -- Generally stable
4. Placement/Device -- LAST priority, most risky (people switch devices during day)

**Decision Rules:**
- Segment must have been running for at least 5-10 days
- Need 30+ conversions per week for that ad set
- Stability already reached before splitting
- Audience sizes: 1M+ great, 10M+ even better, more than that perfect

**H-Scale Split Execution (CBO):**
- Keep up to 5-7 ad sets per CBO (ideal 5)
- If one segment breaks out (e.g., ROAS 3.5x vs target 2x) -> pull into separate campaign with higher budget
- When splitting: either pause original (to avoid auction overlap) or let run if audience is tens of millions
- Effectively doubles spend without V-scaling

**DCO Strategy within H-Scale:**
- Duplicate winning segment 3 times in new campaign
- Each ad set gets a DCO (Dynamic Content Optimized) ad with ONE angle only
- Example: fitness supplement with angles = weight loss / muscle building / feeling good
- Allows testing multiple angles while scaling

**Critical Warning:** "Duplicating winning ad sets post-2019 works far less than it used to. Use with caution."

---

### Strategy 4: H-Scale Dupe (Horizontal Scale - Duplicate)

**Definition:** Simply duplicate winning campaigns to higher budgets. Higher risk.

**Key Insight:** Post-2018, Facebook changed to favor ad set history much more. New duplicates start with zero data and must relearn. Less reliable than before.

**Rules for H-Scale Dupe:**
- Original should have 50+ conversions per week at campaign level
- Running for several weeks with stability
- Start duplicated campaigns at 9am
- Ideally launch Monday (gives time to settle during week)

**Stop-Loss Rules (Ecommerce):**
- Pause ads with no sales after 2X CPA target
- Pause ads higher than 2X CPA once spend hits 3X CPA
- Pause ads higher than 1.25X CPA once spend hits 5X CPA
- Pause ad sets higher than 2X CPA once spend hits 10X CPA
- "Double buffer" -- balances giving Facebook enough data vs minimizing losses

**Recovery Protocol:**
- Restart paused ads/ad sets at 6am next day
- Run cycle for up to 7 days
- If by day 4 (72-96 hours) results still bad -> duplicate CBO again (bad traffic pockets)

---

### Strategy 5: M-Scale (Manual Bidding Scale)

**Depesh's Preference Order:** Bid Cap > Cost Cap > Target Cost > ROAS/Value Bid

**When to use M-Scale:** "When speed of scaling is most important OR if I feel Facebook is holding me back due to poor performance in my funnel out of my control."

**Auction Model:**
- Bid x Estimated Action Rate + User Value = Auction outcome
- Higher bids reach premium users (higher conversion rate, higher value) but at higher CPM
- CPM penalty applied if ad account scores are low
- "As your costs go up, often your click-through rate goes up and your conversion rate goes up as well" (reaching better users)

**eBay Analogy for Bid Cap:**
- Like eBay max bid: you set a ceiling, Facebook auto-adjusts up to it
- Don't bid insanely high -- you'll win auctions you shouldn't win and overpay

#### M-Scale: Bid Cap (Depesh's #1 manual bid preference)
- Duplicate best audience into separate CBO manual bid test campaign
- Set BID CAP at 1X, 2X, 3X, 4X your CPA target in separate ad sets
- Total CBO budget = (Bid1 x 5) + (Bid2 x 5) + (Bid3 x 5) + (Bid4 x 5)
- MIN budget per ad set = CPA x 2
- Optional MAX budget per ad set = CPA x 5 (remove after day 3)
- Run 2-4 ads per ad set (use shared Post IDs for copies)
- **"Usually between 2X and 3X bids that work well on average"**

**Bid Cap Ad-Level Pause Rules (Lower Risk):**
- Spend > 2X CPA with no conversions -> PAUSE
- Spend > 4X CPA and CPA over 50% of target -> PAUSE
- Spend > 6X CPA and CPA over 25% of target -> PAUSE

**Bid Cap Ad-Level Pause Rules (Higher Risk):**
- Spend > 4X CPA with no conversions -> PAUSE
- Spend > 8X CPA and CPA over 50% of target -> PAUSE
- Spend > 12X CPA and CPA over 25% of target -> PAUSE

**Bid Cap Ad-Set Level Pause Rules (Lower Risk):**
- Spend > 5X CPA with no conversions -> PAUSE
- Spend > 5X CPA and CPA over 25% of target -> PAUSE
- Spend > 10X CPA and CPA higher than target -> PAUSE

#### M-Scale: Cost Cap
- Only run if 100+ profitable conversions already
- Test at 75%, 100%, 125% of CPA target in 3 separate ad sets
- Winner usually sits between 75% and 100%
- Expect lower delivery than Bid Cap -- "better if profitability is more important than volume"

#### M-Scale: Target Cost
- Best for short conversion paths (lead gen, mobile app installs)
- Test same 75%, 100%, 125% method
- Most stable day-to-day but lowest volume
- "If your ad set is failing to deliver, it's usually because your bid is too low"

#### M-Scale: ROAS/Value Bid
- Requires multiple purchase values
- Best with Highest Value/Lowest Cost (auto) combo
- Set ROAS ranges around target +/- 25%

### Scaling Example Results (Real Account)
- $35K spend in one month
- Subscription e-commerce, target CPA < $80, cost per account creation ~$30
- Broad campaigns + DCO (Dynamic Content Optimized) outperformed lookalikes/interests
- Auto bid outperformed all manual bid strategies for this account
- Duplicate (H-Scale Dupe) did NOT perform as well as original
- Bid Cap testing: best performance at 2-3X CPA bid level
- Final setup: broad campaigns, DCO ads, auto bid, V-scaled to $1,500/day -- "almost hands off"

---

## MODULE 26: BUSINESS SCALING

### Three Growth Levers (The Only Three)
1. **More Customers** -- Acquire new people into your funnel
2. **Higher Spend** -- Get existing customers to spend more (higher AOV)
3. **Repeat Purchases** -- Get customers to buy again

### More Customers -- Strategies
1. **Go Global** -- International expansion into untapped markets with lower costs/higher conversion rates (Netherlands, Italy cited as examples)
2. **New Ad Account for Time Zones** -- When expanding into different regions (e.g., US -> Europe)
3. **Traffic Expansion** -- Never rely 100% on Facebook. Include Google ads, YouTube, Pinterest, affiliate marketing, PR, blogs, owned media.
- "If your business is 100% reliant on Facebook and anything goes wrong -- you lose your business manager, your ad account, even your personal profile -- and 100% of your revenue relies on Facebook, that's a problem."

### Repeat Purchases -- The Reverse Sales Funnel
At purchase, customer is HOT. Over time without repurchase: warm -> cold. Invert the full funnel system for existing customers.

**Post-Purchase Timeline:**
- First 30 days are critical
- Thank you video (email or Facebook ads)
- Keep product/service front of mind
- Get into email flow for re-engagement

### RFM Model (Recency, Frequency, Monetary Value)
Maximize value from existing customers through:
- **Word of Mouth** -- Get happy customers to tell friends/family
- **Incentives** -- "Buy again within 3 days for 25% off"
- **Loyalty** -- After 3rd purchase, get one free
- **Subscription** -- Convert one-time buyers to recurring revenue

### Subscription Heuristic
"Some people will be happy making a loss on the first purchase. When running ads for subscription, we make a loss on that first purchase but work on the back end to make profit after purchase 3, 4, 5."

## L2 MENTAL-MODELS

- MM-DM-001: "Facebook Auction = Day Trading Model â€” The Facebook auction is unpredictable, like currency trading. There's speculation, profit and loss, risk and reward, external market forces. Know when to cut losses and when to scale up." | domain: scaling-mindset | source: 02-Mindset_MP4.md

- MM-DM-004: "Risk-Reward 2x2 Matrix â€” Beginner (low risk, low reward) -> Controller (low risk, high reward) -> High Roller (high risk, high reward). Avoid bottom-right quadrant (fast scale, low reward)." | domain: scaling-mindset | source: 02-Mindset_MP4.md
- MM-DM-005: "CPA = CPC / Conversion Rate. There are only two components that make up cost per acquisition â€” cost per click and conversion rate. Influence those independently." | domain: metrics-analysis | source: 02-Take_Control_of_CPA___ROAS_MP4.md
- MM-DM-006: "ROAS = AOV / CPA. Return on ad spend is made up of average order value and cost per acquisition. To improve ROAS, either increase AOV or reduce CPA." | domain: ecommerce-metrics | source: 02-Take_Control_of_CPA___ROAS_MP4.md
- MM-DM-007: "Maslow's Hierarchy applied to ad creative â€” The lower on the pyramid (basic needs like food, water, sleep), the easier to communicate, shorter funnel. The higher (self-esteem, confidence), the more storytelling needed, longer funnel." | domain: creative-psychology | source: 03-N2_-_Engage.md
- MM-DM-008: "Audience Objective Crossover Model â€” Within a 10M audience, Facebook isolates subgroups: likely to buy, likely to view video, likely to click, likely to engage. - MM-DM-009: "Product Awareness Matrix â€” 4 quadrants: (1) Aware of solution + Aware of product = price/promotion focus; (2) Aware of solution + Unaware of product = product demos; (3) Unaware of solution + Aware of product = convince them; (4) Unaware of both = hardest, need full storytelling." | domain: creative-strategy | source: 03-N2_-_Engage.md
- MM-DM-010: "Nudge Theory applied to advertising â€” Influence via non-coercive behavior. Get your way without force. Like putting fruit at eye level (nudge) vs banning junk food (mandate)." | domain: persuasion | source: 01-3N_Ads_Intro_MP4.md
- MM-DM-011: "Ogilvy's Objection Smashing â€” Write down all customer barriers. What are they likely to say no to? Attack their flaws and have a defence for every objection. Attack is the best form of defense." | domain: copywriting | source: 04-N3_-_Convert_MP4.md
- MM-DM-012: "Inverted Pyramid for Ad Copy â€” Few words at top (headline/hook), expand with more words (subheadline), then chunky information, then main content. Mirror newspaper structure." | domain: copywriting | source: 03-N2_-_Engage.md
- MM-DM-013: "Excitement Peaks Model for post-purchase â€” Peak 1: moment of purchase. Peak 2: item arrives in hand. Peak 3: first 2-4 weeks of using product. Time your upsell promotions to these peaks." | domain: lifetime-value | source: 02-Take_Control_of_CPA___ROAS_MP4.md
- MM-DM-014: "The Pulse of the Account â€” Every account, every site reacts differently. Even two very similar e-commerce businesses react in different ways. Learn the pulse before scaling aggressively." | domain: account-management | source: 04-Creative_Fishing_Strategy_MP4.md
- MM-DM-015: "Insurance Policy Model â€” Keep original winning ad set as insurance at original budget. Push all risk to duplicate ad sets. If duplicates fail, original is still running." | domain: scaling-strategy | source: 02-The_H_Scaling_Strategy_MP4.md
- MM-DM-016: "Compound Scaling Illusion â€” 20% increments per day doesn't sound like a lot, but compound increase over 5 days = 300%. Frame it as controlled 300% growth, not reckless doubling." | domain: scaling-strategy | source: 01-The_V_Scaling_Strategy_MP4.md
- MM-DM-017: "Staircase Scaling Method â€” Increment 20% every other day until confident, then 20% twice per day. First thing morning based on yesterday, mid-afternoon based on today. Plus 10% reduction as punishment signal." | domain: scaling-strategy | source: 01-The_V_Scaling_Strategy_MP4.md

## L5 METHODOLOGIES

- MET-DM-001: "Graduation Framework Implementation â€” Step 1: Research audiences (5 methods). Step 2: Create PPE campaign, one interest per ad set at $5-10/day. Step 3: Run 3 different creatives per ad set. Step 4: Measure CTR and CPC (not cost per engagement). Step 5: Also check downstream metrics (add to cart, product views). Step 6: Graduate winners to main WC/LG campaign using post ID to keep social proof. Step 7: Group winning interests by compatibility when moving to main campaigns." | domain: testing | source: 03-Part_2_-_Testing_MP4.md, 04-Creative_Fishing_Strategy_MP4.md
- MET-DM-002: "Creative Fishing Strategy Implementation â€” Step 1: Pick best proven audience. Step 2: Create PPE campaign at $5/day per ad set. Step 3: Test 3 ads per ad set (different formats: static, carousel, video + different copy variants). Step 4: Measure CTR, CPC, and downstream metrics (add to cart, video views). Step 5: Pause below-average ads, bring in new variants. Step 6: Keep winners in PPE building social proof. Step 7: Duplicate winning ads to WC campaign via post ID. Step 8: Use format x copy matrix (e.g., 4 formats x 3 copies = 12 variants to test)." | domain: creative-testing | source: 04-Creative_Fishing_Strategy_MP4.md
- MET-DM-003: "Ads Sniper Optimization Process â€” Step 1: Go to Ads Manager, select last 3 days. Step 2: At campaign level, identify campaigns above/below CPA target. Step 3: Drill into worst-performing campaign, sort ad sets by amount spent. Step 4: Compare each ad set's CTR, CPC, CPA against campaign average. Step 5: Drill into problem ad set, compare ad-level metrics against ad set average. Step 6: Pause ads below ad set average. Step 7: For winning ad sets, also optimize â€” look for ads performing 2x+ better than average and push those. Step 8: Check for negative comments on failing ads." | domain: optimization | source: 01-Ads_Sniper_Strategy_Example_MP4.md
- MET-DM-004: "V-Scaling Staircase Method â€” Step 1: Identify ad set with proven CPA below target. Step 2: Increase budget 20% first thing in morning (based on yesterday's data). Step 3: Wait at least one full day. Step 4: If metrics remain good, increment another 20%. Step 5: When super confident, do 20% twice/day (morning + mid-afternoon max 2-3 PM). Step 6: If CPA drifts up, decrease 10% as punishment signal. Step 7: Monitor CPC trend â€” if CPC rises but CR also rises, you're reaching higher-quality users. Step 8: Refresh creatives when frequency rises." | domain: scaling | source: 01-The_V_Scaling_Strategy_MP4.md
- MET-DM-005: "H-Scaling Deployment Method â€” Step 1: Identify consistently performing ad set. Step 2: Create 2-10 exact duplicates. Step 3: Change ONE variable per duplicate: bid strategy (auto vs manual), bid level (1x, 2x, 3x CPA), placement (break out Instagram vs Facebook), device (mobile vs desktop), landing page. Step 4: Launch early in the day. Step 5: By 1 PM, if duplicates aren't near winning ad set benchmarks, pause them. Step 6: V-scale any winning duplicates. Step 7: Keep original ad set at original budget as insurance." | domain: scaling | source: 02-The_H_Scaling_Strategy_MP4.md
- MET-DM-006: "4 Funnel System Setup Process â€” Step 1: Name campaigns by funnel level (COLD-WC-ProductName, WARM-VV-BrandVideo, HOT-WC-Retarget, EXIST-WC-Upsell). Step 2: Set up exclusions (COLD excludes WARM, HOT, EXISTING; WARM excludes HOT, EXISTING; etc.). Step 3: For new account, start COLD only. For existing account, fix HOT retargeting first. Step 4: As cold traffic fills funnel, set up warm audience campaigns. Step 5: Match creative intensity to funnel position (value at top, direct response at bottom, testimonials/urgency in retargeting). Step 6: Search 'campaign equals cold' for filtered analysis." | domain: account-structure | source: 01-The_4_Funnel_System_-_YouTube_MP4.md
- MET-DM-007: "3N Ad Construction Process â€” Step 1 (N1 Stop the Scroll): Pop colors, use color contrast, try grayscale for lifestyle shots, use spacing/cropping, make visuals slightly unnatural, create strong video thumbnails. Step 2 (N2 Engage): Lead with emotional appeal, then functional benefits. Use inverted pyramid copy (headline -> expand -> detail). Test controversy, storytelling, customer states. For long copy, nail the first 2 lines. Step 3 (N3 Convert): Add explicit CTA ('Pick yours now, hit the sign up button'), build social proof, add urgency, test different CTA buttons per funnel stage (softer for cold, direct for hot)." | domain: ad-creation | source: 02-N1_Stop_The_Scroll.md, 03-N2_Engage.md, 04-N3_Convert.md
- MET-DM-008: "Manual Bid Testing Protocol â€” Step 1: Run auto bid until CTR and CR are stable and satisfactory. Step 2: Use H-scaling to create duplicates. Step 3: Test Lowest Cost Cap at 1x CPA target. Step 4: Test at 2x CPA target. Step 5: Test at 3x CPA target. Step 6: Start each at $100/day. Step 7: V-scale the winning bid level. Step 8: Once best cap found, test Target Cost at that level and slightly below for sustained scaling. Step 6: Create dedicated landing pages for winning products (not standard Shopify product pages). Step 7: A/B test landing pages using tools like VWO, Unbounce, ClickFunnels. - MET-DM-010: "Post-Purchase Revenue Maximization â€” Step 1: Set up one-time offer immediately after first purchase (25-30% off higher-end product). - MET-DM-011: "STAR Testing Workflow â€” Step 1 (Setup): Drop test idea into backlog with descriptive name ('Test New HR Audiences 2.3'). - MM-DM-202: "Facebook Algorithm = Advertiser Bid x Estimated Action Rate + User Value = Total Value in the Auction. Winner isn't the ad with the highest monetary bid but the ad that creates the most total value." | domain: facebook-algorithm | source: 03-The_Facebook_Algorithm_UNLEASHED.md
- MM-DM-203: "The 5W Avatar: What (product), Who (target), Wow (emotional/intangible benefits), Why (functional benefits), Why Not (objections). Each emotional line becomes a different angle to test." | domain: avatar-construction | source: 03-Building_Your_5W_Avatar.md
- MM-DM-204: "4-Funnel System: Cold (never heard of you) â†’ Warm (engaged but not clicked) â†’ Hot (clicked into site) â†’ Existing (already purchased). Creative matches the intent/position of the user." | domain: funnel-architecture | source: 02-BPM_Method.md
- MM-DM-205: "ROAS = Average Order Value / Cost Per Acquisition. CPA = Cost Per Click / Funnel Conversion Rate. These cascading metrics show what's in your control and what's harder to manipulate." | domain: kpi-cascading | source: 02-KPIs___Goal_Setting.md
- MM-DM-206: "3N Ads Formula (3-Nudge): N1 = Stop the Scroll (thumb-stopping), N2 = Engage (keep them there, hook interest), N3 = Convert (get the action). Nudge = influence via non-coercive behavior." | domain: ad-creation | source: 01-The_3N_Ads_Formula.md

- MM-DM-208: "Graduation Framework 3 Phases: Phase 1 = Find winning audiences (3-5 audiences for 2-3 months runway), Phase 2 = Find winning ads (ads fatigue faster than audiences), Phase 3 = Stabilize, optimize, scale (main campaigns)." | domain: testing-methodology | source: 01-Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework.md
- MM-DM-209: "The Profit/Revenue Curve: Testing (losses) â†’ Profitability â†’ Stability â†’ Scaling (may lose profitability) â†’ Re-stabilize at higher spend â†’ Growth. Revenue growth doesn't always match profit growth." | domain: growth-trajectory | source: 01-The_Profit_or_Revenue_Curve.md
- MM-DM-210: "Three R's of Content: Relationship (I'm here for you â€” matchmaking via graduation framework), Rapport (I understand you, I hear you), Relevance (I know what you need â€” right ad at right time)." | domain: content-strategy | source: 02-Uncovering_Your_Avatar_s_Needs_and_Desires.md

## L3 HEURISTICS

- HEUR-DM-202: "Lower value product = shorter funnel. $25 product doesn't need multi-step video sequence. Higher value ($200+) = longer funnel with more touchpoints and more time between them." | domain: funnel-design | source: 02-BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-203: "For CBO: don't go more than 5-7 ad sets per campaign with 3-4 ads per ad set. Going beyond that introduces too many variables and starts to destabilize." | domain: campaign-structure | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md

- HEUR-DM-207: "For small CBO budgets ($50/day): minimize variables. Cannot run 5 ad sets with 5 ads ($50 for 25 variables won't work). Run 2 ad sets with 2 ads = 4 variables." | domain: small-budget-cbo | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md
- HEUR-DM-208: "If you have only one winning audience in CBO, duplicate it 2-3 times. CBO can't cope well with not having enough variables. Even identical duplicates perform better than single ad set." | domain: cbo-structure | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md
