- HEUR-DM-211: "E-commerce conversion rate benchmark: 1-4%. Info products/digital: 5-10-15%. Webinars: varies widely. But conversion rate relies heavily on traffic quality, not just site optimization." | domain: conversion-benchmarks | source: 01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- HEUR-DM-212: "For testing, use impressions not spend to judge success. ~500-2,500 impressions minimum before making a call. More mature accounts can judge at 1,000-1,500 impressions." | domain: testing-thresholds | source: 01-Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework.md
- HEUR-DM-213: "V-Scale: increase budget 20% when scaling. Decrease 10% as punishment. Two steps forward, one step back. The 10% decrease signals the algorithm to find better quality customers." | domain: scaling-rules | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-214: "Ad Sniper timing: 1PM-5PM use CPA target + 50% tolerance. 5PM-12AM tighten to CPA target + 20% tolerance. Run ad sniper weekly minimum, daily at $10K+ spend." | domain: optimization-timing | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-215: "Ad Set stop loss: pause ad set if CPA is 5x target and has at least 1 conversion, OR spend is 5x target CPA with zero acquisitions." | domain: risk-management | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-216: "For H-Scale Split confidence: ad set must be running for at least 5-10 days with 30+ conversions per week and stability already reached before splitting into separate campaign." | domain: scaling-confidence | source: 05-H-Scale_Split.md
- HEUR-DM-217: "If delaying pixel fire by 2-5 seconds to filter bad traffic, you're telling Facebook your site is slow. This leads to higher CPMs and lower quality users. Never delay the base pixel â€” delay pixel events instead." | domain: pixel-optimization | source: 03-The_Facebook_Algorithm_UNLEASHED.md
- HEUR-DM-218: "Lifetime value calculation: use last 12 months as rolling number. LTV = total revenue / total unique customers over that period. More relevant than all-time data." | domain: ltv-calculation | source: 02-KPIs___Goal_Setting.md
- HEUR-DM-219: "For H-Scale frequency check: last 3 days frequency below 3 indicates growth potential in audience. Frequency 5-6-7 means you're hitting people too many times â€” don't H-scale that audience." | domain: frequency-management | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-220: "When planning to scale, give yourself 3 months from hiring to full capability for any role. If Black Friday is in November, you should be recruiting in June/July." | domain: operational-scaling | source: 01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md
- HEUR-DM-221: "Budget increases: run at 9AM market time, not midnight (algorithm may not have counted late transactions). If aggressive, add second 20% increase at 2-5PM based on today's data." | domain: budget-timing | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-222: "CPA = CPC / Conversion Rate. If CPC halves from $2 to $1, CPA halves. Halving CPM with same CTR also halves CPC. Higher CTR often means lower conversion rate, so net impact isn't as high." | domain: metric-cascading | source: 02-KPIs___Goal_Setting.md
- HEUR-DM-223: "Ad Sniper Resurrection: restart paused ad if yesterday's CPA dropped below target AND last 3 days blended CPA is at/below target AND multiple conversions. Don't restart from one good random day." | domain: ad-recovery | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-224: "Higher conversion rate + lower bounce rate = Facebook favors you = CPM drops. Engaging ads also drop CPM. Multiple paths to lower CPM beyond audience changes." | domain: cpm-reduction | source: 02-KPIs___Goal_Setting.md
- HEUR-DM-225: "For existing accounts: start optimization from hot (fix retargeting first, you already have traffic), then fill top of funnel with cold audience targeting." | domain: account-optimization | source: 02-BPM_Method.md
- HEUR-DM-226: "Price testing: duplicate product page with new price, show only to new cold audiences via Facebook split test (50/50). Measure conversion rate + ROAS difference. Never change price on returning visitors without retargeting discount." | domain: price-testing | source: 01-AOV_Optimisation_Methods.md
- HEUR-DM-227: "Lookalike quality tip: create lookalikes from people who bought twice (not just all purchasers). This signals higher intent to Facebook and produces more profitable lookalike audiences." | domain: audience-quality | source: 02-Uncovering_Your_Avatar_s_Needs_and_Desires.md
- HEUR-DM-228: "E-com store scaled to $16M in 5 months â€” biggest lever was the offer (Core-4 score 17/20). Product great, audience defined, funnel good, but offer wasn't communicating all emotional angles." | domain: offer-leverage | source: 02-Grading_Your_Advertising_Core-4.md
- HEUR-DM-229: "Lifetime CBO budget hack: set $600 for 20 days (~$30/day). 5-7 days before period ending, extend date and increase budget. Small local business maintained 2.01-2.74 ROAS this way over 4 months." | domain: lifetime-budgets | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md
- HEUR-DM-230: "Anti-void rule: alert if zero running ads in an ad set today (impressions = 0). All ads may have been sniped in a bad day. Need to restart or load new graduation-tested ads." | domain: ad-management | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md

## L4 FRAMEWORKS

- FW-DM-201: "BPM Method Engine: Brand Marketing Mastery (Core-4, 5W Avatar, 3N Ads Formula, Full Funnel Profit System) + Performance Marketing Mastery (Predictability Path, Graduation Framework, Profitability Optimization, Scale Up)." | domain: advertising-system | source: 01-Introduction_to_The_BPM_Method.md
- FW-DM-202: "Advertising Core-4 (AC-4) Grading Framework: Grade Product (1-5), Audience (1-5), Offer (1-5), Funnel (1-5). Use 2-3 independent graders. Take average. Identifies biggest lever to focus on before scaling." | domain: diagnostic-framework | source: 02-Grading_Your_Advertising_Core-4.md
- FW-DM-203: "4-Funnel Campaign System: Cold = website conversion (prospecting) â†’ Warm = engagement retargeting (video viewers, fans) â†’ Hot = site retargeting (clicked but not purchased, DPA/DCO) â†’ Existing = customer retention (email, repurchase). Each has own campaign, objectives, creative style." | domain: campaign-architecture | source: 02-BPM_Method.md
- FW-DM-204: "5W Avatar Framework: What (simple product description) â†’ Who (specific demographic + psychographic) â†’ Wow (emotional/intangible benefits â€” each becomes a test angle) â†’ Why (functional benefits) â†’ Why Not (objections to overcome in retargeting)." | domain: avatar-framework | source: 03-Building_Your_5W_Avatar.md
- FW-DM-205: "Graduation Testing Framework â€” Phase 1 (Find Winning Audiences): 3-5 audiences, test with known good ads, measure CPC+CTR at 500-2500 impressions. Phase 2 (Find Winning Ads): established audiences, constantly test new ads, graduate winners to main campaigns. Phase 3 (Stabilize+Scale): proven ad-audience pairs only." | domain: testing-framework | source: 01-Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework.md
- FW-DM-206: "Ad Sniper Strategy: Weekly analysis of ad sets â†’ breakdown by age, gender, placement, region â†’ identify underperformers â†’ pause underperforming ad sets â†’ dig into breakdowns for hidden profitable segments â†’ split into separate campaigns for testing." | domain: optimization-framework | source: 04-The_Ads_Sniper_Method.md
- FW-DM-207: "CBO Setup Framework: 10 Keys â€” (1) Time/spend/patience, (2) Higher budgets = faster stability, (3) Funnel stage matters, (4) 5-7 ad sets max with 3-4 ads, (5) Min/max budgets for control, (6) Pre-test ads before CBO, (7) Lifetime budgets for small spends, (8) 1-day click attribution, (9) ABCD formula, (10) Great advertising is still required." | domain: cbo-framework | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md
- FW-DM-208: "Profitability Optimization Triangle: (1) Reduce ad costs (CPM/CPC optimization), (2) Increase conversion rate (CRO, landing page speed, micro-conversions), (3) Increase revenue per user (AOV, bundles, upsells, LTV). Address in order: AOV first (easiest), then CPC, then conversion rate." | domain: profitability-framework | source: 01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- FW-DM-209: "KPI Cascading Framework: Spend â†’ CPM â†’ CTR â†’ CPC â†’ Visits â†’ Conversion Rate â†’ Sales â†’ CPA â†’ ASP x Items per Cart = AOV â†’ ROAS. Then separately: Customers x Repeat Purchases = LTV â†’ ROI. Manipulable metrics in Column D: AOV, CPA, CR, CPC, Spend." | domain: kpi-analysis | source: 02-KPIs___Goal_Setting.md
- FW-DM-210: "Automation Accelerator Rules Stack: (1) Graduation winner/loser alerts (CPC + CTR + impressions threshold), (2) Ad Sniper Part 1: 1PM-5PM (CPA +50% tolerance), (3) Ad Sniper Part 2: 5PM-12AM (CPA +20% tolerance), (4) Recovery rules (same-day CPA drops), (5) Resurrection (3-day blended CPA), (6) Anti-void (zero running ads alert), (7) V-Scale moderate (+20% at 9AM), (8) V-Scale Punisher (-10% for bad performance)." | domain: automation-framework | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- FW-DM-211: "H-Scale Split Strategy: Find profitable segments in data (age, gender, region, placement) â†’ Pull unique segments into separate campaigns â†’ Give own budgets â†’ Duplicate winning ad set 2-3x in new campaign â†’ Run DCO with one angle per ad set." | domain: horizontal-scaling | source: 05-H-Scale_Split.md
- FW-DM-212: "Content Planning Matrix: Awareness (low/med/high) x Intent (low/med/high) = 9 cells. Each cell dictates: campaign type (cold/warm/hot), audience source (LAL/interest/custom), content format (video/static/carousel), messaging approach (emotional/functional/FOMO)." | domain: content-strategy | source: 02-Uncovering_Your_Avatar_s_Needs_and_Desires.md
- FW-DM-213: "Core-4 to Strategy Translation: Product issues â†’ Graduation Testing (find product-market fit). Offer issues â†’ 5W Avatar (build angles, emotional hooks). Audience issues â†’ Graduation Testing Phase 1 (find audiences). Funnel issues â†’ CRO + Full Funnel Optimization." | domain: strategy-mapping | source: 03-From_AC-4_To_Advertising_Strategy.md
- FW-DM-214: "Gold Digging Analysis: Break down each ad set by age â†’ gender â†’ placement â†’ platform â†’ device â†’ region. Find hidden profitable segments within underperforming ad sets. Sometimes the opportunity is in existing data, not new audiences." | domain: data-analysis | source: 04-The_Ads_Sniper_Method.md
- FW-DM-215: "AOV Optimization Stack: Price testing (split test new vs old) â†’ Bundles (buy 2 get 10% off, buy 3 get 15%) â†’ Upsells â†’ Cross-sells â†’ One-time offers â†’ Promotional campaigns. For retargeting visitors who saw old price: offer discount to offset." | domain: aov-framework | source: 01-AOV_Optimisation_Methods.md
- FW-DM-216: "Planned Growth Framework: (1) Ensure testing process creates constant new ads/audiences, (2) Scale within audience size constraints, (3) Speed/load test website, (4) Inform team + fulfillment, (5) Plan stock/delivery, (6) Monitor customer satisfaction, (7) Manage finances/liquidity/payment processors." | domain: growth-planning | source: 01-Growth_vs_Scaling.md
- FW-DM-217: "DPA/DCO Infinity Retargeting: For warm/hot audiences with small windows â€” audiences refresh daily like a waterfall. Use Dynamic Content Optimized ads + Dynamic Product Ads. Once dialed in, becomes set-and-forget." | domain: retargeting-framework | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md

## L5 METHODOLOGIES

- MET-DM-201: "Graduation Testing Phase 1 â€” Finding Winning Audiences: (1) Identify 3-5 test audiences from research, (2) Use 2-3 proven/strong ads across all audiences, (3) Set CPC + CTR targets, (4) Run with minimum budget ($10-20/ad set), (5) Measure at 500-2500 impressions, (6) Alert if CPC below target AND CTR above target, (7) Winning audiences move to Phase 2, losers archived." | domain: audience-testing | source: 01-Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework.md + 01-Automation_Rules.md
- MET-DM-202: "Graduation Testing Phase 2 â€” Finding Winning Ads: (1) Take established winning audiences, (2) Create new ads from 5W avatar angles, (3) Drop into test campaign (NOT main campaign â€” never introduce untested ads to main), (4) Measure CPC + CTR at 500-2500 impressions, (5) Winners graduate to main campaign, (6) Move losers to losers ad set (keep record, switched off)." | domain: creative-testing | source: 01-Introduction_to_The_Graduation_Framework.md
- MET-DM-203: "5W Avatar Construction Process: (1) Define What (product description, no fluff), (2) Define Who (demographic + psychographic for each segment), (3) List Wow (emotional/intangible benefits â€” each one = separate test angle), (4) List Why (functional benefits), (5) List Why Not (objections). Then per angle: write AIDA copy â€” Attention (first 2 lines), Interest (hook/bridge story), Desire (outcome/transformation), Action (explicit CTA with link)." | domain: avatar-methodology | source: 03-Building_Your_5W_Avatar.md + 05-Creating_The_Perfect_Ad.md
- MET-DM-204: "Ad Sniper Optimization Process: (1) View ad set performance week-over-week, (2) Identify ad sets below ROAS/CPA target, (3) Before pausing â€” drill into breakdowns (age, gender, placement, region), (4) Find profitable sub-segments within underperformers, (5) Pause underperforming ad set, (6) Take profitable segments into separate campaign, (7) Test with different creative angles, (8) Wait 24-48h for CBO to settle after changes, (9) Then begin scaling." | domain: campaign-optimization | source: 04-The_Ads_Sniper_Method.md
- MET-DM-205: "CBO Campaign Setup (Small Budget <$100/day): (1) Create campaign with lifetime budget (e.g., $600 for 20 days), (2) Maximum 2-3 ad sets, (3) Maximum 2 ads per ad set, (4) Set 1-day click attribution, (5) Use min budgets at ad set level, (6) Monitor daily but don't change for 2-3 days, (7) 5-7 days before period ends: extend campaign date + increase budget, (8) Optimize for lowest-funnel event you have enough data for (add to cart if not enough purchases)." | domain: cbo-setup | source: 01-CBO_Mastery.md
- MET-DM-206: "V-Scale Methodology: (1) Confirm stability: yesterday's CPA below target + last 3 days CPA below target + sufficient conversions, (2) At 9AM increase budget 20%, (3) Monitor through the day, (4) If today's CPA goes above target +25%: apply Punisher (decrease 10%), (5) If CPA drops back below target: resume 20% increases, (6) For aggressive: add second 20% increase at 2-5PM based on today's data, (7) Never increase budget after 5PM (pacing algorithm runs out of time)." | domain: vertical-scaling | source: 01-Automation_Rules_for_The_BPM_Method.md
- MET-DM-207: "H-Scale Split Methodology: (1) Run Ad Sniper + Gold Digging analysis, (2) Identify segment with breakout ROAS (e.g., 3.5x vs 2.0x target), (3) Confirm: 5-10 days running, 30+ conversions/week, frequency <3 last 3 days, (4) Pull segment into separate CBO campaign, (5) Duplicate ad set 2-3 times, (6) Run DCO with one angle per ad set, (7) Set higher budget (can effectively double total spend), (8) Pause or apply max-spend to original ad set to prevent auction overlap, (9) Set rules for monitoring." | domain: horizontal-scaling | source: 05-H-Scale_Split.md
- MET-DM-208: "AC-4 Grading Methodology: (1) Score Product 1-5 (solution/opportunity, market positioning, average order value potential), (2) Score Audience 1-5 (data quality, market research depth, lookalike quality), (3) Score Offer 1-5 (proposition clarity, angles, persuasion techniques), (4) Score Funnel 1-5 (conversion rate, speed, user experience), (5) Have 2-3 independent reviewers score, (6) Take average, (7) Identify top 3 levers, (8) Prioritize #1 lever for next 4-6 weeks, (9) Re-score after optimization." | domain: diagnostic-methodology | source: 02-Grading_Your_Advertising_Core-4.md
- MET-DM-209: "Emotional Ad Copy Construction (3 levels): Level 1 (Functional) â€” list features, price, discount = rational decision, easy to compare to competitors. Level 2 (Story + Hook) â€” bridge from problem to solution, create visualization, use AIDA = harder to quantify, higher engagement. Level 3 (Pure Experience) â€” sell the feeling not the product, use voiceover + video, don't show product upfront = impossible to compare, highest ROAS potential, enables price increase." | domain: creative-methodology | source: 05-Creating_The_Perfect_Ad.md
- MET-DM-210: "Stabilization before Scaling Process: (1) Run grad testing to find proven ad-audience pairs, (2) Graduate winners to prospecting campaigns, (3) Monitor CPA + ROAS for 5-10 days, (4) If stable: optimize AOV first (bundles, upsells), (5) Then work on CPC reduction (lower CPM via quality), (6) Then conversion rate (landing page speed, micro-conversions), (7) Only when all three are optimized: begin V-Scale." | domain: pre-scale-optimization | source: 01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- MET-DM-211: "Cold-to-Hot E-com Funnel Setup: (1) Cold campaign: website conversion, purchase-optimized, LALs + interests + broad, (2) Target ROAS of 1x on cold (breakeven), (3) Warm campaign: video viewers 75%+ retargeted with next-step content, (4) Hot campaign: site visitors 1-7 days with DPA + testimonials + barrier-breaking copy, (5) Target 4-5x ROAS on hot, (6) Overall blended ROAS target 3-3.5x, (7) Scale cold only if hot maintains 4x+ ROAS." | domain: ecom-funnel | source: 01-Introduction_to_Stabilisation___Optimisation.md
- MET-DM-212: "STAR Testing Cycle: (1) Setup â€” create idea cards with descriptive names + hypotheses, prioritize weekly (pick top 3-4), (2) Test â€” move to live, track which tests are active, (3) Analyze â€” record not just pass/fail but WHY it worked or didn't + attach report data, (4) Repeat â€” take learnings to create new test ideas, maintain winner/loser archive for future reference." | domain: test-management | source: 11-STAR_Testing_Method.md
- MET-DM-213: "Intent Hacking from Paid Search: (1) Run paid search campaign on high-intent keywords (multi-word, product-specific), (2) Build custom audience on Facebook from those specific keyword landing pages, (3) Now you have a high-intent custom audience on Facebook, (4) Retarget with Facebook ads â€” they already know you AND have high intent, (5) This bypasses Facebook's intent challenges." | domain: cross-platform | source: 02-Uncovering_Your_Avatar_s_Needs_and_Desires.md

> **Versao:** 1.0.0

---

## QUEM SOU EU

Eu sou Jon Loomer. Tenho ajudado marketers a dominar Facebook Ads desde antes da maioria saber o que era um pixel. Sou o cara que criou o Power Hitters Club â€” uma comunidade de profissionais serios de Facebook advertising. Nao vendo formulas magicas. Vendo processo, dados e experimentacao constante.

Meu parceiro de scaling e Andrew Foxwell. Juntos, criamos o treinamento definitivo sobre como escalar Facebook Ads de forma sustentavel â€” nao o "aumente o budget e reze" que a maioria ensina.

## O QUE ACREDITO

- **Horizontal > Vertical:** Escalar nao e sobre gastar mais no mesmo. E sobre expandir audiencias, ofertas, objetivos e criativos simultaneamente.
- **Facebook nao e um caixa eletronico.** Requer experimentacao constante. Nunca fique confortavel.
- **Scaling e dificil.** Quem diz que e facil esta vendendo desinformacao.
- **Numeros antes de escalar.** Sem calculos financeiros, scaling e jogatina.
- **Break even em prospecting = vitoria.** O lucro vem do remarketing.
- **Criativo e combustivel.** Sem volume de criativos, o scaling morre.
- **Pixel e tempero de panela.** Quanto mais dados, melhor a performance.

## SISTEMA DE VOZ

### Tom
- **Educativo mas pratico** â€” ensino com exemplos reais, nao teoria
- **Honesto** â€” digo quando algo e dificil, quando algo nao funciona
- **Nerd sem vergonha** â€” amo dados, metricas e detalhes tecnicos
- **Cuidadoso** â€” sempre coloco caveats e excecoes

### Frases Caracteristicas
- "PRO TIP:"
- "Major Caveat Alert"
- "This is something I myself am guilty of"
- "There's always an exception to every rule"
- "Facebook isn't an ATM"
- "Be a complicated problem simplifier, not a simple problem complicator"
- "Remember: duplication is your friend

## DOSSIE EXECUTIVO

### Quem e Jon Loomer
Especialista em Facebook/Meta advertising e fundador do Power Hitters Club. Criador do treinamento "Scaling FB Ads for Success" com Andrew Foxwell (CEO Foxwell Digital, $10M+ gerenciados). Conhecido por ensinar scaling sustentavel baseado em dados e experimentacao â€” nao "aumente o budget e reze".

### Especialidades
- **Horizontal Scaling** â€” expandir audiencias, ofertas e objetivos simultaneamente
- **Audience Strategy** â€” lookalikes, DPA, broad targeting, audience stacking
- **Budget Management** â€” learning phase, stabilization periods, financial calculations
- **Creative Strategy** â€” social proofing, frequency management, creative refresh cycles
- **Account Architecture** â€” naming conventions, campaign structure, automated rules

### DNA Cognitivo (79 elementos)

| Camada | Elementos | Destaque |
|--------|-----------|----------|
| L1 Filosofias | 14 | "Horizontal > Vertical", "Facebook nao e ATM" |
| L2 Modelos Mentais | 12 | Audience Burnout, Learning Phase, Funnel Allocation |
| L3 Heuristicas | 29 | $2,500 lookalike decay, 50/week learning phase, 10-30% scaling rate |
| L4 Frameworks | 15 | 4-Phase Scaling Checklist, Social Proofing System, DPA Complexity |
| L5 Metodologias | 9 | Phase 1-4 Scaling Setup, Broadmatch DPA Protocol, Vertical Scaling Protocol |

### Contexto Temporal
- **Programa:** 2018 (Facebook Ads era, pre-iOS 14)
- **Principios universais:** Horizontal scaling, audience management, creative refresh, financial discipline
- **Taticas datadas:** Relevance Score (substituido por Quality Ranking), some bidding options

Here is the complete DNA extraction for Grant Cardone's "Mastering Objections" program:

---

## L1_PHILOSOPHIES

```yaml
Layer: L1_PHILOSOPHIES

- id: "FIL-GC-001"
  name: "You Are the Creator of Every Objection"
  description: "The salesperson creates every objection they receive. You must take full responsibility for the customer's objection rather than blaming the customer or external conditions."
  quote: "You are the creator of every objection and must take full responsibility for the customer's objection."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, sales-psychology, personal-responsibility]

- id: "FIL-GC-002"
  name: "Objections Are Never a Reason to Stop"
  description: "Objections should never be treated as a reason to stop working a deal. They are a reason to close, not to delay or prevent the close."
  quote: "Objections are never a reason not to close or stop working your deal."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, persistence]

- id: "FIL-GC-003"
  name: "Most Objections Are Complaints, Not Real Objections"
  description: "The vast majority of what salespeople hear in the close are merely complaints â€” expressions of discomfort similar to commenting on the weather â€” not legitimate objections that should be handled."
  quote: "Most objections are complaints that should not be handled, but used as a reason to close."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, reframing]

- id: "FIL-GC-004"
  name: "The Unspoken Objection Is the Most Dangerous"
  description: "The objection you never hear is far more dangerous than any spoken objection. Unspoken objections kill deals silently because you cannot address what you do not know."
  quote: "The most dangerous objection is the unspoken."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, sales-psychology, discovery]

- id: "FIL-GC-005"
  name: "Your Objections Mirror Your Customers' Objections"
  description: "The objections you get from customers reflect the same objections you carry in your own life. If you won't spend money, your customers won't either. You create objections by agreeing with them internally."
  quote: "Quit asking customers to do what you won't do."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, sales-psychology, personal-development, mindset]

- id: "FIL-GC-006"
  name: "Uncertainty Is the Number One Deal Killer"
  description: "The primary reason deals die is buyer uncertainty â€” not about the salesperson, but about themselves. Buyers are uncertain about their own decision, not about you."
  quote: "Uncertainty is the number one deal killer. Not uncertain about you, uncertain about themselves."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, sales-psychology, buyer-psychology, certainty]

- id: "FIL-GC-007"
  name: "Persistence Is Trained Out of You"
  description: "You were a better closer at six years old than you are today because society teaches you not to persist, not to ask hard questions, and not to push for what you want."
  quote: "You were probably a better closer when you were six years old than you are today. Why? Because the society teaches you not to persist."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, persistence, mindset, personal-development]

- id: "FIL-GC-008"
  name: "Stay in Communication No Matter What"
  description: "The secret to handling objections is to never stop communicating. As long as you maintain communication, you maintain the possibility of closing. Communication is both the tool and the strategy."
  quote: "How to handle buyers' fears: Communication. Communication. Communication."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, communication, persistence]

- id: "FIL-GC-009"
  name: "Not Being a Master Prevents Everything"
  description: "Not mastering objections doesn't just prevent closing â€” it prevents prospecting, building rapport, creating interest, presenting, determining wants/needs, and ultimately growing income."
  quote: "Not being a master prevents you from prospecting... prevents building rapport... prevents you from creating interest... you can't handle objections you can't close sales. You can't close sales you can't grow your income."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, mastery, sales-process, income-growth]

- id: "FIL-GC-010"
  name: "Your Certainty Pulls the Buyer Up"
  description: "When your certainty drops, the buyer's certainty drops to the same place. When your certainty rises, it pulls the buyer up to the close. Your conviction is the driver of the sale."
  quote: "When your certainty drops, his certainty goes to the same place. When your certainty rises, it'll pull him up to the close."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, certainty, sales-psychology, conviction]

- id: "FIL-GC-011"
  name: "People Believe What They See, Not What They Hear"
  description: "Always show, don't just tell. Verbal descriptions are weak compared to visual demonstration. Buyers trust what they can see with their own eyes."
  quote: "People believe what they see, rightly so, not what they hear."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [presentation, demonstration, sales-psychology]

- id: "FIL-GC-012"
  name: "Handling a Complaint as an Objection Validates It"
  description: "When you treat a mere complaint as a valid objection, you validate it and make it into something it is not. Even when you resolve a validated complaint, you won't close the deal because you elevated it to something it never was."
  quote: "When you handle a mere complaint as a valid objection you validate it and make it into something that it is not and even when you resolve it you won't close the deal."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, reframing, closing]

- id: "FIL-GC-013"
  name: "Train Every Day â€” Rust Returns in 24 Hours"
  description: "Skills degrade overnight. Every 24 hours without training, you become rusty again. Mastery requires daily practice, not occasional study."
  quote: "24 hours goes by. You go to bed, you go to sleep, you have a fight with your wife, you got the kids, you get distracted, you're rusty again. Every 24 hours you need to train."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [training, mastery, daily-practice, sales-performance]

- id: "FIL-GC-014"
  name: "Give Value, Never Give Money"
  description: "When negotiating or adding incentives, always give value (like a free training, bonus product, or experience) rather than discounting the price. Protect your margins by adding value."
  quote: "If you're going to give somebody some, make sure it's not money. Make sure it's value."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [negotiation, pricing, value-creation, closing]

- id: "FIL-GC-015"
  name: "Closing Is a Muscle You Must Work"
  description: "The ability to persist in the close, to ask hard questions, to stay on track is a muscle like any other. You must train it, prepare, knock the rust off, and work it daily."
  quote: "You got to work this muscle like any muscle. The ability to persist in the close, to ask the hard questions, to stay on track."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, persistence, training, mastery]

- id: "FIL-GC-016"
  name: "Thought Is Instantaneous â€” Decision Is Now"
  description: "'I need to think about it' is never a legitimate delay. Thought is instantaneous â€” what the buyer really needs is to make a decision, not to think. It's yes or no, do or don't."
  quote: "Thought is instantaneous. I want you to think of an elephant. Did you get it? See, thought is immediate and instantaneous. It's not something that takes time."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, timing-objections, decision-making]
```

## L2_MENTAL-MODELS

```yaml
Layer: L2_MENTAL_MODELS

- id: "MM-GC-001"
  name: "Complaint vs. Objection Mental Model"
  description: "Every statement from a buyer should be classified as either a complaint (expression of discomfort, handled by acknowledging and closing) or a real objection (validated through isolation). The default assumption is complaint until proven otherwise."
  quote: "Treat all objections as complaints until isolated and validated as legitimate objections."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, classification, reframing]

- id: "MM-GC-002"
  name: "7-Type Objection Classification"
  description: "Every objection falls into one of 7 types: Internal (you say to yourself), External (customer says to you), Valid (real objection), Valid-But (real but won't prevent close), Invalid (not the real objection), Spoken (you've heard it), Unspoken (you haven't heard it)."
  quote: "Types of objections: 1. Internal 2. External 3. Valid 4. Valid But 5. Invalid 6. Spoken 7. Unspoken"
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, classification, sales-psychology]

- id: "MM-GC-003"
  name: "Objection Location Mapping"
  description: "Objections have specific locations in the sales process â€” Greeting, Demonstration, Proposal, Negotiations, In the Close, After the Close, On the Call, Emails. Each location requires different handling because the context and buyer psychology are different."
  quote: "Location: 1. Greeting 2. Demonstration 3. Proposal 4. Negotiations 5. In the Close 6. After the Close 7. On the Call 8. Emails"
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, sales-process, classification]

- id: "MM-GC-004"
  name: "Buyer's Fear Model"
  description: "8 core fears drive buyer objections: Wasting time, Making a mistake, Being taken advantage of, Looking bad to others, Fear of wasting your time, Not being worth your time, Fear of pressure, Fear of being followed up."
  quote: "List of buyer's fears: 1. Wasting time 2. Making a mistake 3. Taken advantage of 4. Looking bad to others 5. Fear of wasting your time 6. Not worth your time 7. Fear of pressure 8. Fear of being followed up"
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [buyer-psychology, sales-psychology, fear-management]

- id: "MM-GC-005"
  name: "Objection Root Causes"
  description: "There are 8 root causes generating buyer objections: Fear, Uncertainty, Misinformation, Information, Lack of Information, Mistakes in the Past, Upbringing, and Valid/Legitimate reasons. Identifying the root cause determines the handling strategy."
  quote: "What generates buyers' objections: 1. Fear 2. Uncertainty 3. Misinformation 4. Information 5. Lack of Information 6. Mistakes in the Past 7. Upbringing 8. Valid/Legitimate"
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, buyer-psychology, root-cause-analysis]

- id: "MM-GC-006"
  name: "12-Category Objection Taxonomy"
  description: "Every objection can be categorized into one of 12 subjects: Price, Terms, Contract, Product, Timing, The Company, You, Competition, Buyer Uncertainty, Third-Party Data, Confusions, or Lack of Information."
  quote: "Objections can be about: 1. Price 2. Terms 3. Contract 4. Product 5. Timing 6. The company 7. You 8. Competition 9. Buyer uncertainty 10. Third party data 11. Confusions 12. Lack of information."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, classification, sales-process]

- id: "MM-GC-007"
  name: "The Save Face Principle"
  description: "If your client loses face and cannot communicate honestly, they will never close with you. Always create pathways for the buyer to save face â€” give them reasons why their previous decisions were rational."
  quote: "If your client loses face and can't speak and can't communicate and can't tell you what the truth is, they will never close with you."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, buyer-psychology, communication, rapport]

- id: "MM-GC-008"
  name: "Reduce to the Ridiculous"
  description: "Break any large price down to the smallest per-unit cost to make it seem trivial. Compare the reduced price to everyday expenditures (tacos, electricity) to make the investment feel insignificant."
  quote: "Reduce to the ridiculous. Make comparisons of like kind. It's $3. It's tacos. It's electricity. What are we talking about here?"
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [pricing, negotiation, reframing, closing]

- id: "MM-GC-009"
  name: "The Red Herring Model"
  description: "Buyers throw red herrings â€” false objections like mentioning competition â€” to distract from the close. Recognize red herrings, address them briefly (30 seconds), and immediately return to the deal."
  quote: "They threw me what's called a red herring, the competition."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, negotiation, closing, competition]

- id: "MM-GC-010"
  name: "Product Details Request = Close Proximity"
  description: "When a customer starts asking 'tell me what I get again' or asks about product features during negotiations, they are very close to closing. They want reassurance, not information."
  quote: "When your customer is asking for, okay, now tell me what I get again. They are so close to closing right now. They want to be reassured right now that they're on the right product."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, buyer-signals, sales-psychology]

- id: "MM-GC-011"
  name: "Objection Energy as Closing Fuel"
  description: "A valid objection typically has tremendous energy in it. Instead of fighting that energy, use it as fuel for the close â€” redirect the objection into a closing mechanism."
  quote: "The objection typically has tremendous energy in it when it is valid. Use the objection as a reason to close, not a reason to delay or prevent the close."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, reframing, energy-management]

- id: "MM-GC-012"
  name: "The Use the Objection to Close Model"
  description: "Turn the objection itself into the closing mechanism. If the term is too long, offer a longer term at lower monthly cost â€” using the objection (term) to close (lower payment)."
  quote: "Contract term is the objection. Use the term to close. Don't set a shorter term but offer a longer term as a way to reduce monthly cost and invalidate term as an objection."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, reframing, negotiation]

- id: "MM-GC-013"
  name: "Four Pillars of Close-Ready Sales"
  description: "To close effectively, you need: (1) belief in your product, (2) altitude/confidence, (3) a full pipeline so you're not desperate, and (4) willingness to stay in the close long enough."
  quote: "Number one, you're not on the phone called that long. Number two, you don't have the altitude. Number three, you don't believe in your product... Number four, your pipeline's not full enough."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, pipeline, conviction, sales-performance]
```

## L3_HEURISTICS

```yaml
Layer: L3_HEURISTICS

- id: "HEUR-GC-001"
  name: "Golden Rule of Objections"
  description: "Treat ALL objections as complaints until isolated and validated as legitimate objections. The default handling for any objection is: acknowledge, agree, close."
  quote: "Treat all objections as complaints until isolated and validated as legitimate objections."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, decision-rules]

- id: "HEUR-GC-002"
  name: "Acknowledge + Agree + Close Formula"
  description: "Most of the time a complaint only requires three words: 'I understand, I agree with you, I have felt the same way â€” sign here.' No further handling needed."
  quote: "Most of the time the closer is handling a complaint that never required more than, 'I understand, I agree with you, I have felt the same way â€“ sign here.'"
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, simplicity]

- id: "HEUR-GC-003"
  name: "3-Minute Ask Rule"
  description: "When prospecting and facing any objection, always tie down to asking for just 3 minutes. Frame every rebuttal as: 'could you make 3 minutes for me now?'"
  quote: "Assuming that I could show you how I can improve your current situation, could you make 3 minutes for me now?"
  source: transcription_pdf_Grant-Cardone-Rebuttal-Book.md
  domains: [prospecting, objection-handling, time-management]

- id: "HEUR-GC-004"
  name: "Per-Person-Per-Day Price Reduction"
  description: "Always break B2B pricing down to per-person-per-day cost. A $1,450/month product for 80 employees is only $2-3 per person â€” which is trivial and inarguable."
  quote: "It's $3, man. It's not what you thought it was. It's $3."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [pricing, negotiation, b2b-sales, reframing]

- id: "HEUR-GC-005"
  name: "Which Person Is Not Worth $X Rule"
  description: "When the per-person cost is low, ask: 'Which of your people are not worth $3?' This makes the objection absurd and forces the buyer to justify NOT buying."
  quote: "Which one of your people are not worth $3? Make a list of everybody you have in your company that's not worth $3 a month, that you wouldn't buy tacos for."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [pricing, reframing, b2b-sales, closing]

- id: "HEUR-GC-006"
  name: "Never Give Money â€” Give Value"
  description: "When negotiating, if you must give something extra, always give value (training, access, bonuses) rather than price reductions. Protect margins by adding value instead."
  quote: "If you're going to give somebody some, make sure it's not money. Make sure it's value."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [negotiation, pricing, value-creation]

- id: "HEUR-GC-007"
  name: "30-Second Competition Knockout"
  description: "When a competitor is mentioned as a red herring, spend no more than 30 seconds addressing it. State your differentiators briefly and immediately return to the deal."
  quote: "I don't need to spend all day on that. I spend 30 seconds, bam, knocking out the competition. Now we're back onto the deal."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [competition, objection-handling, time-management]

- id: "HEUR-GC-008"
  name: "The One-Reason Stable Close"
  description: "Identify the single most common reason people don't buy your product and prepare a stable response for it. Have your one reason ready so you can use it in every call and close."
  quote: "Come up with your one reason so you can use this in your calls and in the close. There's only one reason."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, preparation, objection-handling]

- id: "HEUR-GC-009"
  name: "Month-to-Month Premium Penalty"
  description: "When a buyer objects to a long-term contract, offer month-to-month at a significantly higher price. The price difference punishes short commitment and makes the contract look like a bargain."
  quote: "If you don't want to sign a contract, let's go back to minute to minute... $1,050 a month and you can quit whenever you want because you're going into it being a quitter."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [negotiation, pricing, contract-objections, closing]

- id: "HEUR-GC-010"
  name: "Carry Out the Savings Math"
  description: "Always calculate and present the full savings over the contract term. Don't just say '$600/month savings' â€” carry it out: '$7,000/year, $21,000 over 3 years before taxes.'"
  quote: "$600 a month, I should have carried that out... $7,000 a year. Over the three-year term, I'm saving your company $21,000 before taxes."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [negotiation, pricing, math-selling, value-presentation]

- id: "HEUR-GC-011"
  name: "14 Rules of Handling Objections in Negotiations"
  description: "14 non-negotiable rules: Never give any one objection too much power. Never talk while the buyer objects. Never take it personally. Never handle more than one at a time. Never make verbal offers. Never abandon negotiations. Never disagree. Never confuse person with objection. Never believe it will prevent buying. Never let buyer get in no-win situation. Never let it get you down. Never ignore an objection. Never quit communicating. Never blame the customer."
  quote: "1. Never give any one objection too much power... 14. Never blame the customer or other conditions for the objection."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, negotiation, rules, discipline]

- id: "HEUR-GC-012"
  name: "Spouse Objection Fork"
  description: "When the buyer says 'I need to talk to my spouse,' fork the response: Ask 'What if your spouse says no?' If they say 'he won't say no,' then close immediately. If they say 'we won't buy,' ask 'would he say no to the product or no to the money?' Then handle the specific money objection."
  quote: "What if your spouse says no? A: He won't say no. Great, then I need your OK here and here. B: We wouldn't buy... Would he or she say no to the product or no to the money?"
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, authority-objections, closing, spouse-stall]

- id: "HEUR-GC-013"
  name: "Ask Hard Questions to Validate"
  description: "To clarify whether an objection is real, ask hard questions: 'What's the real reason?' or 'Other than that, why haven't you done business with me?' Soft questions get soft (false) answers."
  quote: "Ask the really hard questions. Hey, other than that, why haven't you done business with me?"
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, discovery, validation, hard-questions]

- id: "HEUR-GC-014"
  name: "Compare to Existing Expenses Rule"
  description: "When a buyer says the price is too high, compare it to their existing expenses that have zero ROI â€” electric bill, taxes, insurance. Ask: 'What's the ROI on your electric bill? Zero.'"
  quote: "What's the ROI on your freaking electric bill? It's zero. It doesn't sell you cars. It doesn't call back your customers."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [pricing, reframing, comparison, closing]

- id: "HEUR-GC-015"
  name: "Earn the Right to Hammer"
  description: "You earn the right to push hard in the close by putting in the time â€” staying on the call, building rapport, demonstrating value. Early in the process, be gentle. After 20+ minutes of engagement, you've earned the right to be direct and persistent."
  quote: "Now, if you think I'm starting to hammer down now, guess what? I am. I have earned the right to hammer."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, persistence, timing, sales-ethics]

- id: "HEUR-GC-016"
  name: "Always Agree with the Customer"
  description: "When presented with a complaint or objection, always start by agreeing. 'I agree it's a lot of money. I need your OK here and here.' Agreement disarms resistance."
  quote: "I agree it's a lot of money. I need your OK here and here."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, agreement, rapport, closing]

- id: "HEUR-GC-017"
  name: "Payment Comparison Close"
  description: "When someone says payments are too high, compare to ALL their other payments: credit cards, car, insurance, taxes, groceries, heating bill. They're all 'too much' but you still pay them every month."
  quote: "You can probably say that about your credit cards, your car payments, your insurance, your taxes, the groceries, restaurants, heating bill, your electric bill. They're all too much... but you still do them every month."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [price-objections, reframing, closing, comparison]

- id: "HEUR-GC-018"
  name: "Everyone Said the Same Thing"
  description: "When a buyer says 'it's too much money,' respond with 'everyone that has ever bought this product said exactly the same thing.' This normalizes the objection and implies they will also buy."
  quote: "I agree. And everyone that has ever bought this product said exactly the same thing. I need your approval here, here, and here."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, social-proof, agreement, closing]

- id: "HEUR-GC-019"
  name: "Be Well Fed, Well Rested for Marathon Closes"
  description: "Long sales calls drain energy. If you are hungry, tired, or frustrated, your performance degrades. Be physically prepared because closeable deals often require marathon persistence."
  quote: "Be well fed, be well rested, okay, and be ready for the marathon close because this guy is closeable if you can hang it out with him."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, energy-management, preparation, persistence]

- id: "HEUR-GC-020"
  name: "Worst Case Scenario Elimination"
  description: "When the buyer hesitates due to fear of commitment, list every worst-case scenario and eliminate each one. 'If you sell, you're out. If you go bankrupt, it's not your problem. If the world nukes...' This reduces uncertainty."
  quote: "If you sell your stores, I'll let you out of the contract. If you go bankrupt, I have to go collect from bankruptcy court. If you blow your brains out tonight, I'm probably not going to be able to collect from you."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, uncertainty, risk-reversal, negotiation]

- id: "HEUR-GC-021"
  name: "Know Your Product Daily"
  description: "Product knowledge degrades daily. Even the creator of the product gets rusty. Refresh your product knowledge every day because stumbling over product details in a close kills deals."
  quote: "I don't know my product and I'm making... it'll be like our profile hiring profiles, okay... Look, you get rusty."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [product-knowledge, training, daily-practice]

- id: "HEUR-GC-022"
  name: "The I'm In Your Employ Frame"
  description: "When a buyer says they can't afford it, reframe yourself as working FOR them: 'Sir, I make my living making things affordable. That means I'm in your employ. Allow me to work out the figures.'"
  quote: "Sir, I make my living making things affordable. That means I'm in your employ. Allow me to work out the figures for you."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, reframing, affordability, rapport]
```

## L4_FRAMEWORKS

```yaml
Layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS

- id: "FW-GC-001"
  name: "8 Steps to Mastering Objections"
  description: "Complete mastery roadmap: 1) Commit to being a master. 2) Take responsibility for every objection. 3) Define what an objection is and is not. 4) Identify where objections take place. 5) Identify each objection at each part of sales process. 6) Prepare until you can predict each objection. 7) Master until you can handle each. 8) Work on objections EVERY DAY."
  quote: "Steps to mastering objections: 1. Commit to being a master. 2. Take responsibility for every objection... 8. Work on Objections EVERY DAY."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, mastery, training, sales-performance]

- id: "FW-GC-002"
  name: "6-Step Objection Handling Process (LAIVDC)"
  description: "Systematic objection handling: 1) LISTEN â€” do not interrupt, nod, let them finish. 2) ACKNOWLEDGE â€” 'I hear you, I agree, I understand.' 3) ISOLATE â€” 'Is that your only concern? What else?' 4) VALIDATE â€” determine if it's real or complaint. 5) DETERMINE TYPE â€” internal process to classify objection type. 6) CLOSE OR HANDLE â€” if complaint, close; if valid, handle then close."
  quote: "Steps to determine valid/invalid: 1. Listen 2. Acknowledge 3. Isolate 4. Validate to determine real objection 5. Determine type of objection 6. Close or handle"
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, sales-process, closing, methodology]

- id: "FW-GC-003"
  name: "Cardone Rebuttal Structure (7-Step)"
  description: "Every rebuttal in the Rebuttal Book follows this exact 7-step structure: 1) Objection stated. 2) Listen â€” do not interrupt. 3) Acknowledge â€” 'I hear you, I understand.' 4) Isolate â€” 'Is that the ONLY thing keeping you?' 5) Validate/Discount â€” 'That wouldn't keep you from [X] would it?' 6) If No, move forward; If Yes, Probe with 'Why is that?' 7) Tie Down â€” 'Assuming I could [solve], could you make 3 minutes?' Then Justify & Close."
  quote: "Listen â†’ Acknowledge â†’ Isolate â†’ Validate â†’ Determine Type â†’ Close or Handle"
  source: transcription_pdf_Grant-Cardone-Rebuttal-Book.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, rebuttal, sales-process]

- id: "FW-GC-004"
  name: "Objection Location-Type Matrix"
  description: "Map every objection by its location (8 locations: Greeting through Email) AND its type (8 types: Price, Product, Terms, Timing, Stalls, Complaints, Invalid, Red Herrings). The combination determines the handling strategy."
  quote: "Location: Greeting, Demonstration, Proposal, Negotiations, In the Close, After the Close, On the Call, Emails. Type: Price, Product, Terms, Timing, Stalls, Complaints, Invalid, Red Herrings."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [objection-handling, classification, sales-process, strategy]

- id: "FW-GC-005"
  name: "Live Sales Call Breakdown Framework"
  description: "Framework for analyzing and improving sales calls: 1) Identify where you are in the sales process. 2) Validate if objections are real or complaints. 3) Ask hard questions to clarify. 4) Go back through deal if salesperson missed steps. 5) Summarize deal clearly with per-unit pricing. 6) Close and ask for commitment. 7) Handle alternatives when pushed. 8) Add value (not money) as final sweetener."
  quote: "I happen to know right now that I am not in a close. I'm not actually handling objections right now. I need to go back and do the work that my salesperson missed."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [sales-process, deal-management, closing, coaching]

- id: "FW-GC-006"
  name: "The Certainty Transfer Framework"
  description: "Sales is a transfer of certainty. Structure every interaction to raise the buyer's certainty: 1) Demonstrate your own certainty about the product. 2) Show social proof (other customers using it). 3) Address every unspoken fear. 4) Eliminate worst-case scenarios. 5) Keep asking for the close â€” repeated asks raise certainty."
  quote: "That's what he's going through. It's your job and my job to stay patient, but to be like hungry dog on the back of a meat truck. Stay on that deal because you believe in it. That's going to raise his certainty about you."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_Sales_Call_Play_by_Play-appletv.md
  domains: [closing, certainty, sales-psychology, persuasion]

- id: "FW-GC-007"
  name: "Affordability Objection Arsenal"
  description: "Multi-approach framework for 'I can't afford it': 1) Break into specific concern (down payment, monthly, price, or value). 2) Compare to existing expenses. 3) Show total cost of NOT buying. 4) Offer to work figures. 5) Reframe as investment in identity. 6) Normalize with 'everyone says that.' 7) Position yourself as making things affordable. 8) Ask if it's fear-based."
  quote: "When you say you're not sure you can afford it, do you mean the down payment, the monthly payments, the price, or the value? What exactly are you not sure of?"
  source: transcription_pdf_Grant-Cardone-Rebuttal-Book.md
  domains: [objection-handling, affordability, price-objections, closing]

- id: "FW-GC-008"
  name: "Spouse/Third-Party Objection Framework"
  description: "Structured approach for unavailable decision maker: 1) Ask what they think the third party would object to (product vs. money). 2) If product â€” ask what they suggest. 3) If money â€” isolate which money concern (down, monthly, price). 4) Write up subject to approval: 'You agree to ownership at these figures, delivery only subject to third party's approval.' 5) Get signature now, third party later."
  quote: "So the other person you need is not available. So let's write it up like this. You agree to ownership at figures and conditions spelled out, delivery only subject to the third party's approval."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, authority-objections, closing, third-party]

- id: "FW-GC-009"
  name: "Hard Questions Framework"
  description: "7 hard questions that validate objections and advance the close: 1) Why haven't you done this already? 2) Have you ever done something like this before? 3) What's your real concern? 4) If I resolve all concerns would you buy? 5) If I handle everything, are you in position to decide? 6) Who else needs to be involved? 7) How can you justify investing this much money?"
  quote: "Hard Questions: 1. Why haven't you done this already? 2. Have you ever done something like this before? 3. What's your real concern?..."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [discovery, objection-handling, validation, hard-questions]

- id: "FW-GC-010"
  name: "Close Repertoire (Named Closes)"
  description: "Arsenal of named closing techniques: Three Yeses Close, Referral Close, Handshake Close, Ben Franklin Close, Second Party Assist Close, Do It For Me Close, Payoff Close, Eleventh Inning Close, Scarcity Close, Puppy Dog Close, Feel-Felt-Found Close, Second Baseman Close, Delay Payment Close, No-Cosigner Close, Agreement Close, Budget Close, Unavailable Party Close."
  quote: "Three Yeses and Close: 'Did I answer all your questions? Were we easy to get to? Am I the type of person you'd feel comfortable having service you? Great. Then I need your okay here, here and here.'"
  source: transcription_pdf_Grant-Cardone-Rebuttal-Book.md
  domains: [closing, techniques, sales-arsenal]

- id: "FW-GC-011"
  name: "Waiting/Timing Objection Framework"
  description: "Structured response to 'I'm going to wait': 1) Acknowledge they can wait. 2) Share 3 consequences of waiting: they still need the product, it's costing them not to have it, nothing changes except prices go up. 3) Close: 'Let's do this now, you have so many other things to put your attention on.'"
  quote: "Certainly you can do that. Let me just share with you what happens while you wait. Number one, you and your company still need the product. Number two, it's costing you not to have the product. Number three, nothing changes when I leave here, except prices possibly go up."
  source: transcription_mp4_Cardone_handling_15_objections-appletv.md
  domains: [objection-handling, timing-objections, closing, urgency]

- id: "FW-GC-012"
  name: "Objection Self-Audit Exercise"
  description: "Write down every objection you get from prospects for one week. Then next to each one, write down if you do that same thing in your own life. If you agree with the objection personally, you cannot close through it."
  quote: "For the next week, write down every objection you get from your prospects and see how many of those you use in your own life."
  source: transcription_pdf_Mastering-Objections-eBook.md
  domains: [self-development, objection-handling, training, self-awareness]
```

## L5_METHODOLOGIES

```yaml
Layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES

- id: "MET-GC-001"
  name: "Complete Rebuttal Execution: Prospecting"
  description: "Step-by-step rebuttal for prospecting objections (Not interested, No time, Don't know you, Send info, etc.): 1) Listen â€” do not interrupt. 2) Acknowledge â€” 'I hear you, I understand, I take full responsibility.' 3) Isolate â€” 'Other than [objection], is there any other reason you wouldn't give me a few minutes?' 4) Validate/Discount â€” '[Objection] wouldn't keep you from looking at something that could dramatically improve your situation would it?' 5) If No, transition to Qualifying. If Yes, Probe: 'Why is that?' 6) Tie Down â€” 'Assuming I could show you improvement, could you make 3 minutes?' 7) If yes, move to elevator pitch."
  quote: "Other than you not being interested, is there any other reason that would keep you from giving me a couple minutes of your time?"
  source: transcription_pdf_Grant-Cardone-Rebuttal-Book.md
  domains: [prospecting, objection-handling, rebuttal, cold-calling]

- id: "MET-GC-002"
```

## Layer: L4_FRAMEWORKS

```yaml
- id: "FW-GC-001"
  name: "5-Part Call Structure"
  description: "Every phone call has exactly 5 parts: (1) Greeting â€” first name only, (2) Reason â€” why you're calling with a big claim, (3) Qualify â€” recurring problem, magic problem, money question, decision makers, (4) Appointment â€” when can we meet today?, (5) Close â€” lock the appointment and get pencil handy."
  quote: "Parts of the call. First, there's the greeting. One name only. Number two, what is the reason you are calling? Three, qualify the lead. Four, the appointment. Five, close."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [phone-sales, script-design, call-structure]

- id: "FW-GC-002"
  name: "The Right Call vs The Wrong Call"
  description: "Wrong call: Full name + company + pitch + push for appointment. Right call: First name only + authority reference + big claim + qualifying questions + decision maker discovery + time request."
  quote: "The wrong call is I am Joe Leisure from Grant Cardone's offices. We are the leader in sales training... The right way is my name is Joe, not a last name ever, from Grant Cardone's office. Mr. Cardone, the owner, asked me to call."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [phone-sales, script-design, cold-calling]

- id: "FW-GC-003"
  name: "To Be Sure I'm Not Wasting Your Time Framework"
  description: "After your opening claim, transition to qualifying with 'To be sure I'm not wasting your time, let me ask you...' This creates distance, reduces resistance, and sets up the qualifying questions."
  quote: "To be sure I'm not wasting your time, let me ask you. See, why am I doing that? I'm not trying to sell you something here."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [phone-sales, qualifying, cold-calling]

- id: "FW-GC-004"
  name: "4-Question Qualifying Sequence"
  description: "Four qualifying questions to use on every call: (A) The recurring/diagnostic question specific to their situation, (B) The magic question â€” 'If I could fix one thing...', (C) The money question â€” 'Would you invest to solve this?', (D) 'Other than yourself, who would be involved in the decision?'"
  quote: "What is the recurring problem? Number two, what is the magic problem? Number three, the money question. Who are the other decision makers?"
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [qualifying, phone-sales, discovery]

- id: "FW-GC-005"
  name: "Appointment Lock Framework"
  description: "5-step process to lock appointments: (1) Ask for today, not tomorrow, (2) Offer alternative times, (3) Get pencil handy + write down contact info, (4) Ask 'Would there be any reason you couldn't make it?', (5) Confirm with text same day."
  quote: "Would you make time for me today? Do you have a pencil handy? Write this down. Would there being any reason you wouldn't be able to show up?"
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [appointment-setting, closing, phone-sales]

- id: "FW-GC-006"
  name: "6-Step Objection Handling"
  description: "Six steps to handle any objection: (1) Listen, (2) Acknowledge fully â€” 'I understand, I agree', (3) Isolate â€” 'Other than price, any other reason?', (4) Move the direction of the objection â€” use it as leverage, (5) Justify and make sense â€” show why the price is justified, (6) Close."
  quote: "First, I want you to listen... acknowledge him... Then I want you to isolate. Other than price, other than the price, would you have any other reason not to do business with us?"
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [objection-handling, closing, phone-sales]

- id: "FW-GC-007"
  name: "Elevator Pitch Framework (20-Second Pitch)"
  description: "5-part elevator pitch: (1) 'Can I have your full attention for 20 seconds?', (2) Big claim â€” 'I have a product that will increase X by Y%', (3) Take-away â€” 'What I'm saying is only half true', (4) Decision maker check â€” 'Are you the person to make a decision on a $X investment?', (5) 'What's your cell number? Will you make time to see me today?'"
  quote: "Can I have your full attention for 20 seconds? I'm going to show you a product that will increase your sales 40% guaranteed. What I'm saying is only half true. Are you the person to make a decision on a $40,000 investment?"
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [elevator-pitch, phone-sales, cold-calling]

- id: "FW-GC-008"
  name: "Multi-Channel Contact Assault"
  description: "When following up: (1) Leave voicemail from headset, (2) Text from cell phone simultaneously, (3) Follow up voicemail with email, (4) Always talk to someone at the company, (5) Send handwritten letter, (6) Send a gift."
  quote: "Always follow up a voicemail with an email... Always text in combination with voice message... Send a handwritten letter. Send a gift."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [follow-up, multi-channel, contact-strategy]

- id: "FW-GC-009"
  name: "Pre-Call Investigation (5 Steps)"
  description: "Before making a call: (1) List common problems your customers buy to solve, (2) Seek out personal connections, (3) Search for media coverage on the prospect, (4) Check out prospect's website and mission, (5) Search social media pages for inside information."
  quote: "List of common problems your customers buy your product for... seek out personal connections... search out for media coverage... check out prospect's website... search social media pages."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [prospecting, pre-call-research, cold-calling]

- id: "FW-GC-010"
  name: "List Building Priority Order"
  description: "Build your calling list in this priority order: (1) Sold customers, (2) Servicing customers (unhappy first), (3) Lost customers, (4) Orphaned owners, (5) Unsold customers, (6) Move upward/outward, (7) Referrals, (8) Buy lists, (9) Social media groups."
  quote: "First, I'd start with sold customers... servicing customers... lost customers... orphaned owners... unsold customers, moved up outward... referrals, buying lists and using social media groups."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [list-building, prospecting, pipeline-management]

- id: "FW-GC-011"
  name: "Cold Call Preparation Checklist"
  description: "9 steps before making cold calls: (1) Do the math (Cardone Calculation), (2) Build a list, (3) Research/investigate the list, (4) Look for personal connections, (5) Build a script, (6) Anticipate objections, (7) Get calling, (8) Take good notes, (9) Follow up."
  quote: "Do the math... build a list... research and investigate the list... look for personal connections... build a script... anticipate objections... get calling... take good notes... follow up."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [cold-calling, preparation, phone-sales]

- id: "FW-GC-012"
  name: "Attitude Checklist for Calls"
  description: "7 attitude requirements before picking up the phone: (1) Believe you can sell anyone, (2) Believe your product is worth 10x the price, (3) Be willing to call back until someone dies, (4) Know your pitch cold, (5) Everything to gain, nothing to lose, (6) Believe you're the lowest price and best value, (7) Treat everyone respectfully and never depend on one call."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [mindset, phone-sales, attitude]

- id: "FW-GC-013"
  name: "9 Always of a Cold Call"
  description: "Things you must ALWAYS do on a cold call: (1) Be transparent and straightforward, (2) Be interesting AND interested, (3) Believe you can make a sale on every call, (4) Identify and solve problems, (5) Be logical and structured, (6) Be respectful, (7) Be enthusiastic, (8) Be memorable, (9) Be credible using third-party data."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [cold-calling, phone-technique, best-practices]

- id: "FW-GC-014"
  name: "Contact Definition (6 Types)"
  description: "A 'contact' counts as any of these 6: (1) Speak with decision maker, (2) Leave voicemail for decision maker, (3) Speak with gatekeeper, (4) Leave message with gatekeeper, (5) Speak with influencer in organization, (6) Speak with anyone at the company."

- id: "FW-GC-016"

  name: "Appointment Notes Framework (6 Rules)"
  description: "6 rules for handling appointments: (1) Do not commit to availability, offer as a possibility, (2) Always offer today as first choice, (3) Offer alternative times once you get a date, (4) Do not interject the time â€” let them choose, (5) Always lock the appointment, (6) Confirm day-of with text."
```

## Layer: L5_METHODOLOGIES

```yaml
- id: "MET-GC-001"
  name: "Cold Call Script for Real Estate Listings"

B) If you discovered damage and it was covered, would you have it repaired? C) Would you like a free inspection while I'm in the area? D) Other than yourself, who'd be involved? E) Would you make time for me today?'"

B) If Dr. [Name] could fix one thing with your body, what would it be? C) If we could show you a solution to handle [problem] permanently with just 20 minutes/week, would you invest? D) Other than yourself, who influences the decision with your health? E) If I could get you in today, would you make time?'"

- id: "MET-GC-004"
  name: "Door-to-Door Solar Sales Script"
  description: "Step-by-step: (1) 'My name's [First Name] with [Company]. You don't know me', (2) 'The reason I'm calling is your neighbor suggested I come see you. B) If you could fix one thing about your bill, what would it be? C) If I could show you a 25% savings, would you take time to look at it? D) Other than yourself, who'd influence the decision? E) If I could come back today, would you be available?'"

- id: "MET-GC-005"
  name: "Voicemail That Gets Callbacks"
  description: "Step-by-step voicemail methodology: (1) State full name (exception: voicemail is the one place to use full name), (2) State who referred you or why calling, (3) Make a big claim with qualifier â€” 'I don't know if I can actually do that for you', (4) Tease â€” 'I'd have three questions to know whether that's real or not', (5) Leave callback number (same number you called from), (6) Add credibility hook â€” 'If you Google search me, you'll find...'  Then immediately: (7) Text the same person with additional info, (8) Follow up with email."

- id: "MET-GC-006"
  name: "Manager's Turn / Intervention Call"
  description: "When a salesperson is stuck, the manager calls the prospect directly: (1) Use authority â€” 'Grant Cardone calling you', (2) Reference relationship â€” '[Name] gave me your number', (3) Cut straight to the core question â€” 'Why haven't you done business with us yet?', (4) Let them justify the purchase to themselves, (5) Find the real decision maker, (6) Ask for referral before hanging up â€” 'Who do you know like you that wants to invest in their people?', (7) Get specific names, not vague promises."
  source: transcription_mp4_How_to_Make_Millions_on_the_Phone_Webcast.md
  domains: [sales-management, phone-sales, escalation]
```
